description: JSON Schema definitions from dr CLI

breadcrumbconfig.title: BreadcrumbConfig
breadcrumbconfig.description: Configuration for breadcrumb navigation display, specifying path generation rules, separator styles, truncation behavior, and home link settings. Applied globally to define breadcrumb rendering for a navigation scope rather than attached per individual Route. Provides users with location context and navigation history.

labelTemplate.description: Template string for dynamic label generation using {title} placeholder syntax to interpolate route metadata. Used when breadcrumb labels need to vary by route context rather than a static label.

contextvariable.title: ContextVariable
contextvariable.description: A typed variable shared across steps of a NavigationFlow, enabling state to persist and transfer between route transitions.

scope.description: Lifetime and visibility of the variable across navigation flow execution.

persistedIn.description: Storage mechanism used to persist the variable across route transitions: sessionStorage (browser session), localStorage (cross-session), memory (in-process only), url-param (serialized into the URL query string).

required.description: When true, the flow cannot proceed if this variable is unset or null. Used to enforce mandatory input collection before advancing to dependent steps.

flowstep.title: FlowStep
flowstep.description: A single ordered node in a NavigationFlow, binding a sequence position to a specific route that the user must visit as part of a multi-step process. Each step carries a sequence index (determines order), a route reference, an optional required flag (whether the step may be skipped), and a waitBehavior governing whether the flow advances immediately or blocks until the step completes. FlowSteps compose into NavigationFlows that realize multi-page business processes such as checkout, onboarding, or wizards.

waitBehavior.description: Execution model governing how the flow waits for this step to complete before advancing.

guardaction.title: GuardAction
guardaction.description: Defines the response executed by a NavigationGuard when its condition evaluates to false (access denied). Specifies whether to redirect the user, block navigation in place, notify with a message, or prompt for confirmation — along with the target destination and whether to preserve the attempted route for post-authentication redirect.

action.description: The action taken when the guard denies access to the target route.

target.description: Destination for redirect or replace actions — a Route ID (e.g., 'login') or an absolute URL. Ignored for block and notify actions.

message.description: User-facing message shown when action is 'notify' or 'prompt', explaining why access was denied.

preserveRoute.description: When true, the originally attempted route is stored (e.g., in session state) so the user can be redirected back to it after successfully authenticating.

guardcondition.title: GuardCondition
guardcondition.description: Boolean predicate evaluated by a NavigationGuard to determine whether route access should be permitted. Expressions may reference user session state, roles, or feature flags. Async conditions (e.g., token validation API calls) are supported with a configurable timeout; on timeout, the condition fails closed (access denied) to preserve security.

expression.description: Boolean predicate expression to evaluate — may be a JavaScript expression, CEL expression, or logical reference to a named predicate (e.g., 'user.isAuthenticated && user.roles.includes(route.meta.roles)').

async.description: When true, the expression is evaluated asynchronously (e.g., via an API call). The guard will await resolution up to the timeout limit before failing closed.

timeout.description: Maximum milliseconds to wait for an async condition to resolve. On timeout, the guard treats the condition as false (access denied) to fail securely.

navigationflow.title: NavigationFlow
navigationflow.description: An ordered sequence of FlowSteps that together realize a multi-step user process — such as a checkout wizard, onboarding tour, or multi-page form — by linking discrete routes into a directed path with defined entry and exit points. A NavigationFlow is the top-level container for a user journey: it groups FlowSteps, imposes traversal order, and provides the orchestration context that connects navigation structure to a business process outcome.

navigationgraph.title: NavigationGraph
navigationgraph.description: Root container for an application's complete routing structure, composing all Route, NavigationTransition, and NavigationGuard nodes into a unified navigation model.

application.description: Reference to the Layer 4 ApplicationComponent that this navigation graph belongs to, enabling cross-layer traceability from routing to application architecture.

navigationguard.title: NavigationGuard
navigationguard.description: A conditional interceptor that evaluates whether a user is permitted to activate a route, applied at route resolution time before the target component renders. Guards encapsulate cross-cutting access policies — authentication state, role membership, feature-flag enablement, or custom predicates — and execute in priority order (lowest order value first) when multiple guards protect the same route. A guard may allow activation, redirect the user, or block navigation entirely based on its evaluation result.

type.description: Classification of the guard mechanism: auth (authentication check), role (role membership), permission (fine-grained permission), feature-flag (feature toggle), custom (arbitrary condition).

navigationtransition.title: NavigationTransition
navigationtransition.description: A directed edge in the navigation graph representing an authorized route change from a source route to a destination route, triggered by a specific mechanism (user click, programmatic dispatch, timeout, browser-back, or redirect). Models the actual paths users traverse between states in the application — distinct from the routes themselves — enabling analysis of navigation flows, dead-end detection, and trigger-based transition policies.

trigger.description: Mechanism that initiates the route transition: user-click (explicit user interaction), programmatic (code-driven), timeout (time-based), browser-back (browser history navigation), redirect (automatic server/guard redirect).

route.title: Route
route.description: A named, addressable destination in the application's navigation graph, representing a URL path pattern and the view or component it resolves to. Routes are the atomic unit of the navigation layer — each route maps a URL pattern to a specific UX artifact and carries an access classification (public, protected, redirect, alias, or lazy-loaded) that governs how the router handles incoming requests. Routes may be composed into NavigationFlows to model multi-step user journeys.

type.description: Access and loading classification: public (no auth), protected (requires auth), redirect (auto-forwards), alias (multiple paths to same component), lazy (code-split, loaded on demand).

routemeta.title: RouteMeta
routemeta.description: Per-route rendering and access configuration attached to a Route node and consumed by the SPA router at runtime. Covers layout selection, component instance caching, animation transitions, breadcrumb labeling, and access control hints. Security attributes (requiresAuth, roles, permissions) are navigation hints that should be enforced through NavigationGuard nodes in the security layer.

requiresAuth.description: Hint indicating this route requires an authenticated session; used by NavigationGuard nodes to enforce access control.

roles.description: Comma-separated list of roles permitted to access this route (e.g., 'admin,editor'). Enforced via NavigationGuard.

permissions.description: Comma-separated list of permission strings required for this route (e.g., 'reports:read,users:write'). Enforced via NavigationGuard.

layout.description: Name or reference of the layout component to wrap this route's view (e.g., 'DefaultLayout', 'AuthLayout').

breadcrumb.description: Breadcrumb label or reference to a BreadcrumbConfig node; used to render the route's position in the location trail.

keepAlive.description: When true, the route's component instance is cached and reused across navigation events rather than destroyed and re-created.

transition.description: Name of the CSS transition or animation applied when entering or leaving this route (e.g., 'fade', 'slide-left').

analyzer.name: cbm
analyzer.description: Tree-sitter-based code knowledge graph server by DeusData

project_identification.description: DR CLI passes the project root as an absolute path via findProjectRoot(). The analyzer uses this path to establish a stable project identity for consistent indexing and caching across sessions.

analyzer.notes: Uses absolute path indexing for project identification. Ensures consistent graph generation and caching across multiple analysis runs within the same project.

Route.description: HTTP route maps to API operation with high confidence

Function.description: Function maps to application function; may be promoted to service via heuristics

Method.description: Class method maps to application function; may be promoted to service via heuristics

Module.description: Module (file/namespace) maps to application component with low confidence

HTTP_CALLS.description: HTTP API call between services maps to consumes relationship
HTTP_CALLS.implementation_note: Requires HTTP verb and endpoint analysis to create cross-layer reference

HANDLES.description: HTTP request handler maps to provides relationship
HANDLES.implementation_note: HTTP handler decoration indicates implementation relationship

REFERENCES.description: Reference to variable, field, or constant

high.description: High confidence: mapping is reliable and well-supported by evidence

medium.description: Medium confidence: mapping is reasonably supported but may have ambiguity

low.description: Low confidence: mapping is tentative and may require manual verification

heuristics.name: min_fan_in
heuristics.description: Function/method with high fan-in (many callers) may be a service
heuristics.rule: If a function/method is called by 5+ other functions/methods, it exhibits service-like behavior and is eligible for promotion to applicationservice

heuristics.name: directory_match
heuristics.description: Element location in canonical service directories indicates service-level component
heuristics.rule: If element resides in directory matching service-level patterns, promote eligible functions to applicationservice

heuristics.name: naming_patterns
heuristics.rule: If function/class name ends with service-indicating suffix (Service, Manager, Repository, etc.), promote eligible functions to applicationservice

heuristics.name: class_is_service
heuristics.description: Class structure and interface patterns indicate service behavior
heuristics.rule: If class implements interfaces, uses dependency injection, or has 3+ public methods, it may represent a service abstraction

heuristics.name: service_class_naming
heuristics.description: Class names following service patterns within classes indicate service methods
heuristics.rule: If method within service-named class uses CRUD or action-oriented prefix, it exhibits service operation behavior

heuristics.name: is_entry_point
heuristics.description: Functions that serve as entry points (main, handler, endpoint) indicate service potential
heuristics.rule: If function name or type suggests it serves as entry point (handler, endpoint, listener), it may represent service operation

heuristics.name: handles_route
heuristics.description: Functions that handle HTTP routes map directly to API operations
heuristics.rule: Function/method decorated with @Route, @Handler, or similar HTTP decorator maps to api.operation with high confidence

heuristics.name: datastore_detection
heuristics.description: Patterns and conventions that identify datastore-related elements (tables, schemas, migrations)
heuristics.rule: If element resides in datastore-related directories or files, or follows datastore naming conventions, identify as data-store or data-model layer element

inference_rules.name: function-promotion-to-service
inference_rules.description: Qualified functions can be promoted to services via heuristic signals
inference_rules.pattern: applicationfunction + (min_fan_in >= 5 OR directory_match OR naming_patterns OR is_entry_point) => applicationservice

inference_rules.name: verify_scope_v1
inference_rules.description: Scope boundary rule: verify_role=primary applies only to api.operation (Route elements) in v1
inference_rules.pattern: verify_role: primary => api.operation only (excludes Function, Method, Class, Module)

filtering_rules.name: confidence_floor
filtering_rules.description: Filter mappings below confidence threshold to reduce noise

filtering_rules.name: test_code_exclusion
filtering_rules.description: Exclude test files and test-related code from mapping

deduplication_rules.name: semantic-duplicate-detection
deduplication_rules.description: Detect and merge semantically equivalent mappings

actioncomponent.title: ActionComponent
actioncomponent.description: Interactive element that triggers actions (button, menu, link, voice command)

type.description: Interactive element kind, per HTML 5.3 button, anchor, and menu element semantics.

channel.description: Delivery channel for which this component is rendered.

style.description: Visual style variant of the action component, per design system button style conventions.

validations.description: Client-side validation rules applied to this component, per HTML 5.3 Constraint Validation API. Each rule specifies a constraint type, threshold value, and user-facing error message.

type.description: Constraint type. 'required': field must not be empty; 'minlength'/'maxlength': string length bounds (value = integer); 'pattern': regex the input must match (value = regex string); 'min'/'max': numeric range (value = number); 'type': expected data type; 'custom': application-defined rule.

value.description: Constraint threshold or parameter (e.g., '8' for minlength, '^[a-z]+$' for pattern).

message.description: User-facing error message shown when this constraint is violated.

actionpattern.title: ActionPattern
actionpattern.description: Reusable action configuration for common user interactions. Defines the trigger, feedback mechanism, and optional target for recurring UX patterns. Example: 'confirm-and-delete' pattern — trigger: click on delete button, feedback: confirmation modal then success toast, targetRef: the item being deleted.

category.description: Functional taxonomy of the action pattern.

trigger.description: Input modality that initiates this action pattern.

feedback.description: User-facing feedback mechanism invoked after the action completes.

targetRef.description: Optional specNodeId of the component or element this action pattern is applied to.

chartseries.title: ChartSeries
chartseries.description: Configuration for a data series within a chart component, specifying data source, visualization type, colors, and legend properties. Defines how data is visualized in charts.

type.description: Visualization type for this data series.

dataField.description: Field name from the bound data source representing the numeric/value axis of this series (e.g., 'revenue', 'count'). For formal data-model binding, use a relationship to the corresponding data-model entity.

categoryField.description: Field name from the bound data source representing the category/x-axis grouping (e.g., 'month', 'region'). For formal data-model binding, use a relationship to the corresponding data-model entity.

color.description: CSS color value for this series (e.g., '#3B82F6', 'var(--brand-primary)'). Overrides any theme-level color scheme for this specific series.

lineStyle.description: CSS border/line style applied to line chart series rendering, per CSS Border specification.

componentinstance.title: ComponentInstance
componentinstance.description: Instance of a LibraryComponent with application-specific configuration

order.description: Render order of this component instance within its parent View or SubView's component list. Lower values render first.

ref.description: SpecNodeId of the ux.librarycomponent this instance is based on (e.g., 'ux.librarycomponent.data-table'). Determines the component type and the set of valid prop keys.

props.description: Key-value map of property overrides applied to this instance. Keys must match prop names declared on the referenced LibraryComponent. Values override the component defaults for this specific usage.

componentreference.title: ComponentReference
componentreference.description: A declarative placeholder in a parent component's named slot, pointing to another component to be rendered there. Unlike ComponentInstance (a concrete rendered occurrence), ComponentReference is a composition declaration that says 'render this component in this slot of the parent', analogous to the HTML slot element.

ref.description: SpecNodeId of the component (ux.librarycomponent or ux.componentinstance) to render in the slot.

variant.description: Optional design variant override for this reference (e.g., 'compact', 'expanded', 'readonly'). Must match a variant defined on the referenced component.

slot.description: Named slot in the parent component where this reference is placed. Slot names are defined by the parent LibraryComponent (e.g., 'header', 'actions', 'footer').

dataconfig.title: DataConfig
dataconfig.description: Configuration for data binding and state management within UI components, defining data sources, transformation pipelines, and update triggers. Manages component data flow.

source.description: Data origin for this binding configuration.

target.description: SpecNodeId of the destination element receiving bound data (e.g., a data-model entity ID or API endpoint ID). Use relationships to formally link to those layers.

path.description: Dot-notation or JSONPath expression selecting a field within the source data (e.g., 'user.profile.name' or '$.items[0].id').

errorconfig.title: ErrorConfig
errorconfig.description: Configuration for error handling and display within UI components, specifying error message formats, retry behavior, fallback content, and user guidance. Ensures consistent error UX.

notificationType.description: Severity category of the notification, aligned with ARIA live region and alert role semantics.

experiencestate.title: ExperienceState
experiencestate.description: Distinct state that the experience can be in (works across all channels)

initial.description: When true, marks this as the starting state of the state machine. Only one ExperienceState per state machine should have initial: true.

channel.description: Delivery channel context for this experience state.

onEnter.description: List of StateAction specNodeIds executed when entering this state (lifecycle: on-enter).

onExit.description: List of StateAction specNodeIds executed when leaving this state (lifecycle: on-exit).

transitions.description: List of StateTransition specNodeIds owned by this state, defining the outgoing edges of the state machine.

enabledComponents.description: List of component specNodeIds that are interactive (enabled) when this state is active. Corresponds to ARIA aria-disabled=false.

disabledComponents.description: List of component specNodeIds that are non-interactive (disabled) when this state is active. Corresponds to ARIA aria-disabled=true.

hiddenComponents.description: List of component specNodeIds that are not rendered when this state is active. Corresponds to ARIA aria-hidden=true or display:none.

layoutconfig.title: LayoutConfig
layoutconfig.description: Configuration for UI layout structure, defining grid systems, responsive breakpoints, spacing rules, and component arrangement patterns. Controls visual organization of the interface.

type.description: CSS layout mode governing element positioning and flow, per CSS Flexbox/Grid specification.

alignContent.description: CSS align-content property value, per CSS Flexbox/Grid specification.

alignItems.description: CSS align-items property value, per CSS Flexbox/Grid specification.

justifyContent.description: CSS justify-content property value, per CSS Flexbox/Grid specification.

justifyItems.description: CSS justify-items property value, per CSS Grid specification.

stickyPosition.description: Anchor edge for CSS position:sticky elements, per CSS Positioning specification.

style.description: Visual presentation style applied to the layout container.

labelPosition.description: Placement of form field labels relative to their input elements, per HTML 5.3 label element positioning patterns.

librarycomponent.title: LibraryComponent
librarycomponent.description: Reusable UI component definition that can be instantiated in multiple UXSpecs

type.description: UI component category used for library organization and component lookup.

category.description: Fine-grained categorization within the component type (e.g., 'data-grid', 'inline-form', 'bar-chart') for library browsing and search.

source.description: Distribution source for this component (npm package, git repository, or internal registry).

type.description: Distribution mechanism.

identifier.description: Package name, git URL, or registry path (e.g., '@org/ui-components', 'https://github.com/org/ui').

props.description: Schema of properties accepted by this component. Each entry defines an accepted prop with its name, type, and whether it is required.

type.description: Data type of the prop value.

required.description: Whether this prop must be provided by consumers.

default.description: Default value as string; coerced to declared type at runtime.

slots.description: Named content slots this component exposes for composition, analogous to HTML slot elements in Web Components.

description.description: What content is expected in this slot.

events.description: Custom events emitted by this component that consumers can listen to.

description.description: When this event fires and what payload it carries.

librarysubview.title: LibrarySubView
librarysubview.description: Reusable, non-routable grouping of components intended to be composed into Views or other SubViews. Unlike View, LibrarySubView has no route and cannot be navigated to directly. It exists purely as a reusable composition unit, analogous to an HTML template element.

category.description: Functional category grouping related sub-views within a library (e.g., 'forms', 'data-display', 'navigation', 'feedback').

components.description: Ordered list of ComponentInstance specNodeIds that constitute this reusable sub-view composition.

stateaction.title: StateAction
stateaction.description: A concrete action bound to a specific ExperienceState lifecycle event. Instances are bound to a state and lifecycle hook; for reusable parameterizable definitions use StateActionTemplate. Examples: scroll-to-top (lifecycle: on-enter), focus-first-field (lifecycle: on-enter), dispatch-analytics-event (lifecycle: on-exit).

action.description: Operation executed during the state lifecycle.

lifecycle.description: State lifecycle hook that triggers this action. 'on-enter': fires when entering the owning ExperienceState; 'on-exit': fires when leaving it; 'on-transition': fires during any outgoing transition from this state.

timing.description: Execution scheduling within the lifecycle hook: 'immediate' runs synchronously before the state activates; 'deferred' runs asynchronously after; 'conditional' runs only when a guard condition is met.

stateactiontemplate.title: StateActionTemplate
stateactiontemplate.description: A parameterizable, reusable definition of an action to execute during state transitions. StateActionTemplate is the definition; StateAction is the concrete usage bound to a specific ExperienceState and lifecycle hook. Example: a 'scroll-to-top' template with a 'targetId' parameter, instantiated as a StateAction on multiple states.

action.description: Operation this template encapsulates. Must match a value from the StateAction action enum.

parameters.description: Configurable parameters that can be supplied when instantiating this template as a StateAction. Each item defines a named input with its expected type and optional default value.

name.description: Parameter identifier used to reference it in the action (e.g., 'targetId', 'routePath').

type.description: Expected data type of the parameter value.

default.description: Optional default value as a string; coerced to the declared type at runtime.

statepattern.title: StatePattern
statepattern.description: Reusable state machine template for common UX interaction flows, composed of named ExperienceStates and transitions. Canonical examples: 'wizard' — multi-step form with back/next/submit transitions; 'optimistic-update' — loading → success/error with retry; 'tab-panel' — N peer states with tab-driven transitions; 'authenticated-route' — unauthenticated → redirect/login → authenticated.

category.description: Functional taxonomy of the reusable state machine pattern.

states.description: Ordered list of ExperienceState specNodeIds comprising this reusable pattern. The first entry is the initial state.

items.description: specNodeId of an ExperienceState node belonging to this pattern.

statetransition.title: StateTransition
statetransition.description: A directed edge in the ExperienceState machine, owned by its source ExperienceState. Specifies the trigger event, destination state, optional guard condition, and side-effect actions. The 'from' state is implicitly the ExperienceState that contains this transition via relationship.

to.description: SpecNodeId of the destination ExperienceState this transition leads to (e.g., 'ux.experiencestate.order-confirmed').

trigger.description: DOM or UX event that initiates this state transition, per HTML 5.3 event model.

actions.description: List of StateAction specNodeIds to execute when this transition fires, in order.

guard.description: Optional boolean guard condition that must evaluate to true for this transition to fire.

expression.description: Boolean expression string evaluated against current state context.

syntax.description: Expression language used to evaluate the guard expression. 'javascript': JS boolean expression (e.g., 'user.isLoggedIn && cart.items.length > 0'); 'jsonpath': JSONPath predicate (e.g., '$.user.role == "admin"'); 'cel': Common Expression Language (e.g., 'user.age >= 18'); 'jsonata': JSONata expression (e.g., '$count(items) > 0').

subview.title: SubView
subview.description: A non-routable UI fragment embedded within a parent View. Two usage modes: (1) Library instance — 'ref' is populated with a ux.librarysubview specNodeId, rendering that reusable fragment; (2) Inline definition — 'ref' is absent and the SubView defines its own composition inline. SubViews are not independently routable; use View for routable pages.

order.description: Render order of this SubView within its parent View's subViews list. Lower values render first (top-to-bottom or left-to-right depending on layout).

ref.description: SpecNodeId of the ux.librarysubview this instance is based on (e.g., 'ux.librarysubview.data-table-with-filters'). When present, this SubView is a library instance; when absent, it is an inline definition.

tablecolumn.title: TableColumn
tablecolumn.description: Configuration for a single column within a data table component, specifying header, data binding, sorting, filtering, and rendering options. Defines table structure and behavior.

filterType.description: Filter operation type available for this column, per HTML 5.3 form input filter patterns.

textAlign.description: CSS text-align value for cell content, per CSS Text specification.

displayType.description: Rendering type that controls how cell values are visually presented.

defaultSortDirection.description: Default sort direction applied to this column when first sorted.

transitiontemplate.title: TransitionTemplate
transitiontemplate.description: Defines reusable animation and transition patterns for state changes, page navigation, or component lifecycle events. Ensures consistent motion design across the application.

animationType.description: Motion animation category applied during this transition.

duration.description: CSS transition duration (e.g., '300ms', '0.5s'), per CSS Transitions specification.

easing.description: CSS timing function controlling the transition's acceleration curve (e.g., 'ease-in-out', 'cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)').

uxapplication.title: UXApplication
uxapplication.description: Application-level UX configuration that groups UXSpecs and defines shared settings

channel.description: Primary delivery channel this application targets. Multi-channel products use one UXApplication per channel, each referencing channel-specific UXSpecs.

experiences.description: List of UXSpec specNodeIds that comprise the user experiences of this application.

theme.description: Design token theme identifier applied application-wide (e.g., 'brand-light', 'brand-dark', 'high-contrast').

designSystem.description: Identifier of the design system or UXLibrary this application uses (e.g., specNodeId of a ux.uxlibrary node).

defaultLocale.description: Default locale for internationalization, as a BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en-US', 'fr-FR', 'ja-JP').

uxlibrary.title: UXLibrary
uxlibrary.description: Collection of reusable UI components and sub-views that can be shared across applications

components.description: List of ux.librarycomponent specNodeIds exported by this library.

subViews.description: List of ux.librarysubview specNodeIds exported by this library.

statePatterns.description: List of ux.statepattern specNodeIds exported by this library.

actionPatterns.description: List of ux.actionpattern specNodeIds exported by this library.

source.description: Distribution source for this library package.

type.description: Distribution mechanism: 'npm' for a published npm package, 'git' for a git repository URL, 'internal-registry' for a private artifact registry.

identifier.description: Package name, git URL, or registry path (e.g., '@org/design-system', 'https://github.com/org/ds', 'registry.internal/ux-lib').

uxspec.title: UXSpec
uxspec.description: Complete UX specification for a single experience (visual, voice, chat, SMS)

experience.description: Delivery modality this spec describes. 'visual': screen-based UI; 'voice': voice assistant interface; 'chat': conversational messaging UI; 'sms': text message-based interaction.

source.description: Reference to the design artifact (e.g., Figma file, Storybook, or internal design system URL) that this spec is based on.

type.description: Design tool or artifact type.

views.description: List of ux.view specNodeIds that comprise the routable screens of this experience.

states.description: List of ux.experiencestate specNodeIds that represent the top-level states of this experience's state machine.

view.title: View
view.description: Routable grouping of components (a complete user experience)

type.description: View display mode, per HTML 5.3 sectioning and dialog element semantics.

accesspattern.title: AccessPattern
accesspattern.description: A documented data access pattern that describes how applications read or write data. Critical for NoSQL data modeling where schema design is driven by access patterns rather than normalization.

patternType.description: Category of access pattern. POINT_READ: single-record lookup by key. RANGE_SCAN: ordered range of records. FULL_SCAN: all records. WRITE/UPSERT/BATCH_WRITE: mutation patterns. AGGREGATION: computed result over records. SEARCH: full-text or filtered search. VECTOR_SIMILARITY: approximate nearest neighbor. GRAPH_TRAVERSAL: multi-hop graph walk. TIME_RANGE/LATEST_VALUE: time-series queries.

targetCollection.description: The collection this access pattern operates on.

keyCondition.description: Key or partition condition for this access (e.g., 'customer_id = :id', 'PK = USER#:userId AND SK BEGINS_WITH ORDER#').

sortKey.description: Sort key expression for composite key patterns (e.g., 'SK BEGINS_WITH ORDER#', 'timestamp BETWEEN :start AND :end'). Used in DynamoDB/Cassandra to define the clustering dimension of the access pattern.

filterCondition.description: Additional filter condition applied after key lookup.

expectedFrequency.description: Expected frequency of this access pattern. Informs capacity planning and index decisions.

consistencyRequirement.description: Required consistency level for this access pattern.

x-source-reference.description: Source code reference using OpenAPI x- extension pattern.

collection.title: Collection
collection.description: A container for records, documents, or entries within a data store. Paradigm-neutral: maps to SQL table, document store collection, key-value bucket/hash, wide-column table/column family, vector collection, time-series measurement/hypertable, graph node label or edge type, and search engine index.

collectionType.description: The paradigm-specific type of this collection. TABLE: relational table. COLLECTION: document store collection. COLUMN_FAMILY: wide-column column family. BUCKET/HASH/SORTED_SET/STREAM: key-value data structures. VECTOR_COLLECTION: vector embedding store. MEASUREMENT/HYPERTABLE: time-series data container. NODE_LABEL/EDGE_TYPE: graph database structural types. SEARCH_INDEX: search engine index.

partitionKey.description: Primary partition key field name(s). Used by wide-column stores (Cassandra partition key), DynamoDB (partition key), time-series (time field), and sharded relational databases.

clusteringKeys.description: Clustering or sort key field name(s) that determine ordering within a partition. Relevant for wide-column stores (Cassandra clustering columns) and DynamoDB sort keys.

ttlDefault.description: Default time-to-live in seconds for entries in this collection. Relevant for key-value stores, wide-column stores, and time-series databases.

validationSchema.description: Reference to a schema definition that validates documents or records. For MongoDB this is the JSON Schema validator; for relational databases, the set of column constraints; for search engines, the mapping definition.

x-source-reference.description: Source code reference using OpenAPI x- extension pattern.

database.title: Database
database.description: A database instance representing a top-level data store deployment. Paradigm-neutral: covers relational databases, document stores, key-value stores, wide-column stores, vector databases, time-series databases, graph databases, search engines, and multi-model systems.

engine.description: Database engine name (e.g., 'PostgreSQL', 'MongoDB', 'Redis', 'Cassandra', 'Pinecone', 'Neo4j', 'Elasticsearch', 'InfluxDB', 'DynamoDB'). Free-text to accommodate any engine without enumeration constraints.

paradigm.description: Primary data model paradigm. RELATIONAL: SQL-based tabular data (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle). DOCUMENT: JSON/BSON document storage (MongoDB, CouchDB, Firestore). KEY_VALUE: simple key-to-value mapping (Redis, DynamoDB, etcd). WIDE_COLUMN: column-family storage (Cassandra, HBase, Bigtable). VECTOR: vector embedding storage and similarity search (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant). TIME_SERIES: time-indexed measurements (InfluxDB, TimescaleDB). GRAPH: nodes and edges with traversal (Neo4j, Neptune). SEARCH_ENGINE: full-text search and analytics (Elasticsearch, OpenSearch). MULTI_MODEL: natively supports multiple paradigms (ArangoDB, CosmosDB, FaunaDB).

deploymentModel.description: How the database is deployed. STANDALONE: single instance. REPLICATED: primary with read replicas. SHARDED: data partitioned across nodes. DISTRIBUTED: fully distributed with no single primary. SERVERLESS: cloud-managed auto-scaling. EMBEDDED: in-process database (SQLite, RocksDB).

eventhandler.title: EventHandler
eventhandler.description: A reactive mechanism that executes in response to data change events. Paradigm-neutral: covers SQL triggers, MongoDB change streams, DynamoDB Streams, Cassandra CDC, Redis keyspace notifications, Elasticsearch watchers, Neo4j APOC triggers, and time-series alerting rules.

handlerType.description: Type of event handler mechanism. TRIGGER: synchronous SQL trigger (BEFORE/AFTER DML). CHANGE_STREAM: asynchronous document change feed (MongoDB, CouchDB). CDC_LISTENER: change data capture consumer (Cassandra, Debezium). KEYSPACE_NOTIFICATION: key event notification (Redis). STREAM_PROCESSOR: stream-based event processor (Redis Streams, Kafka Connect). WATCHER: periodic condition check and alert (Elasticsearch). ALERT_RULE: threshold or anomaly alerting (time-series, monitoring). CUSTOM: engine-specific mechanism not listed.

targetCollection.description: The collection or database-level scope this handler is attached to.

timing.description: When the handler fires relative to the event. BEFORE: fires before the operation (SQL BEFORE trigger). AFTER: fires after the operation (SQL AFTER trigger). INSTEAD_OF: replaces the operation (SQL INSTEAD OF trigger for views). ON_CHANGE: fires when a change is detected (change streams, CDC). CONTINUOUS: always-on stream processing.

events.description: Events that activate this handler. SQL: INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE. Document: INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/REPLACE/DROP/INVALIDATE. Key-value: EXPIRE/SET/DEL. Monitoring: THRESHOLD/ANOMALY. CUSTOM: engine-specific event not listed.

granularity.description: Granularity of handler activation. ROW: fires once per affected row (SQL FOR EACH ROW). STATEMENT: fires once per SQL statement. DOCUMENT: fires per changed document (document store change streams). KEY: fires per key operation (key-value notifications). BATCH: fires per batch of changes (CDC, stream processing).

action.description: Reference to the stored logic (function name, pipeline reference, or inline expression) that executes when this handler fires.

condition.description: Optional filter condition. Only events matching this condition activate the handler. Maps to SQL WHEN clause, MongoDB pipeline $match stage, or Elasticsearch watcher condition.

field.title: Field
field.description: A named data element within a collection. Paradigm-neutral: maps to SQL column, document store field/path, key-value hash field, wide-column column, vector dimension or metadata field, time-series tag or field, graph property, and search engine mapping property.

dataType.description: Data type of this field. Interpretation is paradigm-specific: SQL types (VARCHAR, INTEGER, TIMESTAMP), BSON types (String, ObjectId, Document), Elasticsearch mapping types (keyword, text, dense_vector), or any engine-native type name.

generated.description: Whether the field value is auto-generated. ALWAYS: value is always system-generated (SQL identity GENERATED ALWAYS, MongoDB auto _id, auto-increment). BY_DEFAULT: system-generated unless explicitly overridden (SQL GENERATED BY DEFAULT). NEVER: value must be user-provided.

fieldRole.description: Semantic role of this field within its collection. VALUE: regular data field. TAG: indexed metadata for filtering (time-series). TIMESTAMP: time-series time field. PARTITION_KEY: wide-column or distributed partition key component. CLUSTERING_KEY: wide-column clustering/sort key component. VECTOR: vector embedding field. METADATA: vector store metadata for filtering. LABEL: graph node label. PROPERTY: graph node or edge property.

vectorDimensions.description: Number of dimensions for vector fields. Only applicable when fieldRole is VECTOR.

distanceMetric.description: Distance metric for vector similarity search. COSINE: angular similarity. EUCLIDEAN: L2 distance. DOT_PRODUCT: inner product similarity. MANHATTAN: L1 distance. Only applicable when fieldRole is VECTOR.

analyzer.description: Text analyzer for search engine fields (e.g., 'standard', 'english', 'keyword', 'whitespace'). Controls tokenization and normalization. Only applicable for search engine paradigm.

nested.description: Whether this field contains nested or embedded documents. Relevant for document stores (MongoDB embedded documents) and search engines (Elasticsearch nested type).

x-source-reference.description: Source code reference using OpenAPI x- extension pattern.

index.title: Index
index.description: A database index for query optimization. Paradigm-neutral: covers B-tree and hash indexes (relational), compound and multikey indexes (document stores), vector approximate nearest neighbor indexes (HNSW, IVF), inverted indexes (search engines), geospatial indexes, and secondary indexes (wide-column stores).

fields.description: Field name(s) included in the index key.

method.description: Index access method. BTREE/HASH: traditional ordered and hash-based (relational, document). GIN/GIST/SPGIST/BRIN: PostgreSQL advanced index types. HNSW/IVF_FLAT/IVF_PQ/ANNOY/LSH: vector approximate nearest neighbor algorithms. INVERTED/FULLTEXT: search engine and full-text indexes. GEOSPATIAL: location-based spatial indexes. COMPOUND/MULTIKEY: document store compound and array indexes. CUSTOM: engine-specific method not listed.

where.description: Partial or filtered index predicate. Only entries satisfying this condition are indexed. Supported by relational (PostgreSQL, SQL Server) and document stores (MongoDB partial index filter).

include.description: Covering index: additional fields stored in the index but not in the search key. Reduces lookups by including frequently accessed fields in the index leaf pages.

sparse.description: Whether the index only includes entries where the indexed field exists. Relevant for document stores with flexible schemas and sparse datasets.

vectorConfig.description: Configuration parameters for vector indexes. Only applicable when method is HNSW, IVF_FLAT, IVF_PQ, ANNOY, or LSH.

dimensions.description: Number of dimensions in the vector space.

efConstruction.description: HNSW build-time parameter controlling index quality vs. build speed tradeoff.

m.description: HNSW maximum number of connections per node in the graph.

nlist.description: IVF number of clusters (Voronoi cells) for partitioning the vector space.

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: A logical grouping of database objects within a database instance. Paradigm-neutral: maps to SQL schema, MongoDB database, Cassandra keyspace, Redis namespace/prefix, Elasticsearch index, vector database namespace, and graph database named graph.

retentionpolicy.title: RetentionPolicy
retentionpolicy.description: A data lifecycle management policy that governs how long data is retained and what action is taken when the retention period expires. Applicable across paradigms: time-series downsampling, key-value TTL, search index lifecycle, relational partition pruning, and archival strategies.

targetCollection.description: The collection or namespace this policy applies to.

retentionDuration.description: How long data is retained, expressed as an ISO 8601 duration (e.g., 'P30D' for 30 days, 'P1Y' for 1 year, 'P6M' for 6 months).

action.description: Action taken when data exceeds the retention period. DELETE: permanently remove. ARCHIVE: move to cold storage. DOWNSAMPLE: aggregate and reduce resolution (time-series). MOVE_TIER: transition to a different storage tier. COMPRESS: apply compression to reduce storage footprint.

downsampleInterval.description: For DOWNSAMPLE action, the aggregation interval (e.g., '1h', '1d'). Only applicable when action is DOWNSAMPLE.

storedlogic.title: StoredLogic
storedlogic.description: Stored computation logic that executes within the data store engine. Paradigm-neutral: covers SQL functions and procedures, MongoDB aggregation pipelines, Redis Lua scripts, Cassandra UDFs, Neo4j stored procedures, Elasticsearch ingest pipelines, time-series continuous queries, and DynamoDB Lambda triggers.

logicType.description: Type of stored computation. FUNCTION/PROCEDURE/PACKAGE: SQL-standard. AGGREGATION_PIPELINE/MAP_REDUCE: document store (MongoDB). LUA_SCRIPT: key-value (Redis). LAMBDA: serverless trigger (DynamoDB Streams). UDF/UDA: user-defined function/aggregate (Cassandra). CONTINUOUS_QUERY/FLUX_TASK: time-series scheduled computation. STORED_PROCEDURE/CYPHER_FUNCTION: graph database. INGEST_PIPELINE/PAINLESS_SCRIPT: search engine. CUSTOM: engine-specific type not listed.

language.description: Implementation language. SQL/PLPGSQL/PLSQL/TSQL: relational database languages. JAVASCRIPT/PYTHON/LUA/JAVA/C: general-purpose embedded languages. CYPHER/GREMLIN/SPARQL: graph query languages. FLUX/INFLUXQL: time-series query languages. PAINLESS/MVEL: search engine scripting. MQL: MongoDB Query Language. EXTERNAL: logic implemented outside the database. CUSTOM: language not listed.

returnType.description: Return type of the computation. For functions, the return data type. For pipelines, the output document shape. For void procedures, 'VOID'.

deterministic.description: Whether the logic always returns the same output for the same input. Generalizes SQL volatility (IMMUTABLE = true, VOLATILE = false) across paradigms. Deterministic logic is a candidate for result caching.

sideEffects.description: Whether the logic can modify data. Functions without side effects are safe for parallel execution and caching. Maps to SQL CONTAINS SQL / MODIFIES SQL DATA distinction.

security.description: Security context for execution. DEFINER: runs with privileges of the creator (SQL SECURITY DEFINER). INVOKER: runs with privileges of the caller (SQL SECURITY INVOKER).

x-source-reference.description: Source code reference using OpenAPI x- extension pattern.

validationrule.title: ValidationRule
validationrule.description: A data integrity or validation rule enforced by the data store. Paradigm-neutral: covers SQL constraints (PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, CHECK, NOT NULL), document store schema validation and required fields, graph uniqueness and existence constraints, key-value key format rules, and search engine mapping enforcement.

ruleType.description: Type of validation or integrity rule. SQL-standard: PRIMARY_KEY, FOREIGN_KEY, UNIQUE, CHECK, NOT_NULL. Document store: SCHEMA_VALIDATION (JSON Schema validator), REQUIRED_FIELD. Key-value: KEY_FORMAT (key naming pattern), TTL_CONSTRAINT. Graph: UNIQUENESS_CONSTRAINT (unique node label+property), EXISTENCE_CONSTRAINT (required property), NODE_KEY (composite unique identifier). Vector: DIMENSION_CONSTRAINT (vector dimension enforcement). Search engine: MAPPING_STRICT (reject unmapped fields). CUSTOM: engine-specific rule not listed.

expression.description: Constraint expression or validation rule body. For CHECK constraints, the SQL expression. For SCHEMA_VALIDATION, a JSON Schema reference. For KEY_FORMAT, a key naming pattern.

targetFields.description: Field name(s) this rule applies to.

referencedCollection.description: For FOREIGN_KEY or cross-collection references, the target collection name.

referencedFields.description: For FOREIGN_KEY or cross-collection references, the target field name(s) in the referenced collection.

onDelete.description: Referential action on delete. CASCADE: delete dependent entries. SET_NULL: nullify the reference. SET_DEFAULT: reset to default value. RESTRICT: reject if referenced entries exist. NO_ACTION: deferred reject. Applicable to FOREIGN_KEY rules.

onUpdate.description: Referential action on update. Same values as onDelete, applied when the referenced field value is modified. Applicable to FOREIGN_KEY rules.

enforcement.description: How the rule is enforced. STRICT: reject violating operations (default for most SQL constraints). WARN: allow but emit a warning (supported by some document store validators). LOG: allow and log the violation for audit purposes.

view.title: View
view.description: A derived or virtual collection that presents data from one or more source collections. Paradigm-neutral: covers SQL views and materialized views, search engine aliases, document store views (CouchDB), time-series continuous aggregates (TimescaleDB), and named graph projections.

definition.description: Query or expression that defines this view. SQL query for relational, aggregation pipeline for document stores, alias definition for search engines, continuous aggregate query for time-series, Cypher projection for graph databases.

materialized.description: Whether the view data is precomputed and stored rather than computed on each read.

viewType.description: Type of view. VIRTUAL: computed on every read (SQL view, CouchDB view). MATERIALIZED: precomputed and stored (SQL materialized view, Cassandra materialized view). ALIAS: pointer or routing abstraction over one or more collections (Elasticsearch alias). CONTINUOUS_AGGREGATE: incrementally maintained materialization (TimescaleDB continuous aggregate, InfluxDB continuous query). FILTERED: read-only subset of another collection.

refreshPolicy.description: How materialized data is refreshed. ON_DEMAND: manual refresh required. ON_COMMIT: refreshed after each transaction commit. PERIODIC: refreshed on a schedule. CONTINUOUS: incrementally updated as source data changes. Only applicable when materialized is true or viewType is MATERIALIZED or CONTINUOUS_AGGREGATE.

contextvariation.title: ContextVariation
contextvariation.description: A distinct test type or environmental context under which a coverage target may be invoked, used to parameterize test design across different execution conditions (functional correctness, load behavior, security posture, regression state). Maps to environmental needs documented in IEEE 829-2008 test design specifications.

contextType.description: The category of test context this variation represents

environmentFactors.description: References EnvironmentFactor nodes specifying the concrete environmental constraints (OS, locale, network, hardware, dependency versions) that define this context variation.

coverageexclusion.title: CoverageExclusion
coverageexclusion.description: A formally approved exclusion of a coverage target or requirement from the test coverage model, documenting the rationale, accepted risk, and accountable approver per test plan scope documentation in IEEE 829-2008.

affectedTargetRef.description: ID of the testing.testcoveragetarget or testing.coveragerequirement being excluded from coverage

dateApproved.description: ISO date when the exclusion was formally approved, supporting audit trail requirements

coveragegap.title: CoverageGap
coveragegap.description: An identified deficit between required and achieved test coverage for a target or requirement, typically surfaced during test summary reporting per IEEE 829-2008. Gaps require remediation, risk acceptance (CoverageExclusion), or deferral.

severity.description: Risk level of leaving this gap unaddressed

affectedRequirements.description: Identifier or reference to the requirement(s) that remain uncovered due to this gap.

remediationStatus.description: Lifecycle state of this coverage gap.

targetRef.description: ID of the testing.testcoveragetarget where this gap was identified

coveragerequirement.title: CoverageRequirement
coveragerequirement.description: A specification of coverage criteria that must be satisfied for a TestCoverageTarget, defining which input partitions, context variations, and outcome categories must be exercised per IEEE 829-2008 test design specification requirements. The specific partitions, context variations, and outcome categories are associated via relationships (to InputPartitionSelection, ContextVariation, and OutcomeCategory nodes) rather than direct attributes.

coverageCriteria.description: The coverage strategy defining which combinations of partition values must be exercised

priority.description: Relative importance of fulfilling this coverage requirement

coveragesummary.title: CoverageSummary
coveragesummary.description: Aggregated coverage status across all targets and requirements within a test coverage model, suitable for test summary reporting.

modelRef.description: ID of the testing.testcoveragemodel this summary aggregates

environmentfactor.title: EnvironmentFactor
environmentfactor.description: A specific environmental variable (OS version, network latency, locale setting, hardware configuration, dependency version) documented as affecting test behavior. Used to parameterize ContextVariation instances with concrete environmental constraints per IEEE 829-2008 environmental needs specification.

factorType.description: Category of environmental variable being documented.

value.description: The specific value or constraint for this factor (e.g., 'Ubuntu 22.04', 'latency < 100ms', 'en-US', 'Node.js 20.x').

expectedRange.description: Acceptable range or set of values for this factor in the test environment (e.g., 'latency 50-200ms', 'Ubuntu 20.04 or 22.04').

testImpact.description: Description of how variations in this factor are expected to affect test behavior or outcomes.

inputpartitionselection.title: InputPartitionSelection
inputpartitionselection.description: A specification of which values from an InputSpacePartition must be exercised within a CoverageRequirement, supporting selective coverage of equivalence classes per IEEE 829-2008 test design specification. When coverAllCategories is true, all partition values are included except those in excludeValues; otherwise, only the values listed in coverValues are included.

partitionRef.description: ID of the testing.inputspacepartition from which values are being selected for coverage.

coverValues.description: Comma-separated labels or IDs of specific partition values to include in coverage. Ignored when coverAllCategories is true.

coverAllCategories.description: When true, all partition values are included in coverage except those listed in excludeValues. Overrides coverValues.

excludeValues.description: Comma-separated labels or IDs of partition values to exclude from coverage. Applied when coverAllCategories is true to carve out specific values.

inputselection.title: InputSelection
inputselection.description: A concrete input value assignment for a TestCaseSketch, binding an abstract partition representative value (selectedValue) to a specific concrete test datum (concreteValue), per test case input specification in IEEE 829-2008.

valueSource.description: How the concrete value was obtained: manually authored, algorithmically generated, drawn from a data set, or loaded from a test fixture

inputspacepartition.title: InputSpacePartition
inputspacepartition.description: Partitioning of an input dimension into testable equivalence classes, where each class groups values expected to behave identically under test. Enables systematic, structured coverage analysis per IEEE 829-2008 equivalence partitioning guidance by ensuring distinct behavioral categories are explicitly identified before test case selection.

presenceRule.description: Whether this input field must be present in test inputs for this partition

outcomecategory.title: OutcomeCategory
outcomecategory.description: An abstract category of observable outcomes (e.g., success, validation error, authorization error, timeout) associated with a TestCoverageTarget, used during test design to organize expected results before test case specification per IEEE 829-2008. Distinguished from specific assertions — OutcomeCategory defines the class of outcome, not the exact expected value.

outcomeType.description: The class of observable outcome this category represents, used to group test cases by expected result type for coverage analysis and reporting.

partitiondependency.title: PartitionDependency
partitiondependency.description: A constraint specifying how selection of a partition value in one field affects valid or available partition values in another field, enabling combinatorial test design to avoid invalid input combinations per IEEE 829-2008 test design specification.

dependsOnPartition.description: ID of the testing.inputspacepartition that triggers this dependency

dependsOnValue.description: Label or ID of the testing.partitionvalue that activates this dependency

effect.description: How the dependency affects the related partition values

affectedValues.description: Comma-separated labels or IDs of the partition values in the dependent field whose availability is modified by this dependency (e.g., values that are excluded, required, or restricted when the trigger value is selected).

partitionvalue.title: PartitionValue
partitionvalue.description: An individual equivalence class within an InputSpacePartition, characterized by a label, representative value, and optional constraint expression, used to enumerate the testable categories of an input dimension per IEEE 829-2008 test design specification.

constraint.description: Human-readable description of the condition this partition boundary represents (e.g., 'must be between 0 and 100', 'must not be null or empty').

constraintExpression.description: Machine-evaluable expression encoding the partition boundary condition (e.g., a regex pattern, JSONPath predicate, or comparison expression such as 'value >= 0 && value <= 100').

category.description: Equivalence partition category for this value

targetcoveragesummary.title: TargetCoverageSummary
targetcoveragesummary.description: Coverage metrics summary for a single test coverage target

targetRef.description: ID of the testing.testcoveragetarget this summary covers

sketchCount.description: Total number of TestCaseSketch nodes for this target (design-time count; superset of implementedCount)

implementedCount.description: Number of sketches with a concrete test case implementation (must be ≤ sketchCount; superset of automatedCount)

automatedCount.description: Number of implemented test cases that are automated (must be ≤ implementedCount)

coveragePercentage.description: Percentage of coverage requirements with at least one implemented test case sketch (0–100)

targetinputfield.title: TargetInputField
targetinputfield.description: An association between a TestCoverageTarget and a specific input field of the target artifact, defining which InputSpacePartition applies to that field and documenting its coverage relevance per IEEE 829-2008 test case specification.

fieldRef.description: ID of the referenced field element in the API layer (e.g., an API parameter) or Data Model layer (e.g., a data-model property)

partitionRef.description: ID of the testing.inputspacepartition that defines the equivalence classes or boundary values for this input field.

relevance.description: Whether this field must be included in test case input specifications

testcasesketch.title: TestCaseSketch
testcasesketch.description: A design-time test case specification that selects concrete input partition values for a CoverageRequirement, serving as the bridge between test design (InputPartitionSelection) and executable test case specification per IEEE 829-2008. Exists in sketch (unimplemented) or implemented status — not yet tied to a specific test runner or execution framework.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the test case sketch

inputSelections.description: References InputPartitionSelection nodes specifying which partition values this sketch exercises.

implementationFormat.description: How the test case will be executed

source.description: Source code reference to the test case implementation

testcoveragemodel.title: TestCoverageModel
testcoveragemodel.description: The root artifact of a testing layer coverage specification, aggregating all TestCoverageTargets, InputSpacePartitions, ContextVariations, CoverageRequirements, and TestCaseSketches for an application. Corresponds to the test plan document structure in IEEE 829-2008, providing a single point of reference for coverage governance.

application.description: Cross-layer reference ID to the application or API element (application layer or api layer) being covered by this model

source.description: Provenance information indicating the origin of this model (e.g., manually authored, imported from OpenAPI, or generated from source artifacts)

testcoveragetarget.title: TestCoverageTarget
testcoveragetarget.description: The primary unit of test scope governance in a TestCoverageModel, identifying an artifact or functionality that requires test coverage per IEEE 829-2008 Section 5.2.3 (Features to be tested). A target may represent an API endpoint, UI component, business workflow, or integration point — each anchoring a set of CoverageRequirements and TestCaseSketches.

targetType.description: The kind of artifact or functionality requiring test coverage

priority.description: Relative importance of achieving coverage for this target

source.description: Source code reference for the artifact requiring test coverage

arrayschema.title: ArraySchema
arrayschema.description: Defines validation rules for JSON array instances, constraining item schemas, cardinality bounds (minItems/maxItems), uniqueness requirements, and contains-subschema matching.

jsonschema.title: JSONSchema
jsonschema.description: The root JSON Schema document, identified by $schema (dialect URI) and $id (base URI for $ref resolution), containing type constraints, annotations, and reusable definitions.

type.description: JSON Schema Draft 7 primitive type

definitions.description: Reusable subschemas keyed by name, referenced elsewhere in the schema via $ref: '#/definitions/{name}'

numericschema.title: NumericSchema
numericschema.description: Defines validation rules for JSON numeric instances (number or integer types), including inclusive/exclusive bounds and divisibility constraints. Note: in JSON Schema Draft 7, exclusiveMinimum and exclusiveMaximum are numeric boundary values (e.g., exclusiveMinimum: 0), not the boolean flags used in Draft 4.

type.description: JSON Schema Draft 7 numeric type: 'number' for any numeric value, 'integer' for whole numbers only

objectschema.title: ObjectSchema
objectschema.description: Defines validation rules for JSON object instances, specifying named properties, required fields, and constraints on additional or dynamically named properties.

properties.description: Named property definitions for the object schema (primary mechanism for defining object structure)

patternProperties.description: Pattern-matched property definitions applied to properties whose names match the given regex

propertyNames.description: Schema applied to all property names of the object instance for name validation

reference.title: Reference
reference.description: A JSON Schema $ref pointer that references another schema by URI or JSON Pointer fragment, enabling schema reuse without duplication. In JSON Schema Draft 7, all sibling keywords alongside $ref are ignored — use allOf to combine a $ref with additional constraints.

$ref.description: URI or JSON Pointer fragment referencing another schema

schemacomposition.title: SchemaComposition
schemacomposition.description: Combines multiple schemas using boolean logic. allOf requires all subschemas to validate; anyOf requires at least one; oneOf requires exactly one; not inverts the result of the subschema.

allOf.description: All listed subschemas must validate against the instance

anyOf.description: At least one of the listed subschemas must validate against the instance

oneOf.description: Exactly one of the listed subschemas must validate against the instance

not.description: The instance must not validate against this subschema

schemadefinition.title: SchemaDefinition
schemadefinition.description: A reusable JSON Schema definition declared under the 'definitions' keyword and referenced via '$ref: #/definitions/{name}'. Enables DRY schema design and consistent type definitions across entities.

type.description: JSON Schema Draft 7 primitive type

description.description: Human-readable explanation of this definition's purpose (JSON Schema Draft 7 annotation keyword)

format.description: Semantic format hint for string-typed definitions (e.g., date-time, email, uri)

enum.description: Restricts the value of this definition to a fixed set of allowed values

examples.description: Sample values illustrating valid instances of this definition (JSON Schema Draft 7 annotation keyword)

schemaproperty.title: SchemaProperty
schemaproperty.description: Defines a single property within a schema, including its type, constraints, validation rules, and documentation. The fundamental building block of data model structure.

type.description: JSON Schema Draft 7 primitive type

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the property's purpose (JSON Schema Draft 7 annotation keyword)

$ref.description: URI reference to a reusable schema definition (e.g., #/definitions/MyType)

stringschema.title: StringSchema
stringschema.description: Defines validation rules for JSON string instances, including length bounds (minLength/maxLength), regular expression patterns, and semantic format hints (e.g., date-time, email, uri). The canonical node type for string type constraints in the data model.

format.description: Semantic format hint for string values (JSON Schema Draft 7 format keyword)

callback.title: Callback
callback.description: Defines a callback where the API sends asynchronous notifications to an external URL derived at runtime. A callback maps a Runtime Expression (e.g., $request.body#/callbackUrl) to a full PathItem object, meaning the callback specifies the complete HTTP interaction — method, headers, and expected response — not just a target URL. Enables event-driven integrations and async workflows.

expression.description: OpenAPI 3.0 Runtime Expression (e.g., $request.body#/callbackUrl) resolved at runtime against the triggering request to determine the callback target URL.

components.title: Components
components.description: A named registry for reusable API definition objects (schemas, responses, parameters, examples, requestBodies, headers, securitySchemes, links, callbacks) that enables $ref referencing throughout the specification. Component entries are not rendered directly — they must be referenced by operations, parameters, or schemas to take effect. Component name keys must match the pattern ^[a-zA-Z0-9.\-_]+$ per OpenAPI 3.0 — arbitrary strings are not valid component identifiers.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Contact information for the API owner or maintainer, including name, email, and URL. Enables consumers to reach out for support or collaboration.

encoding.title: Encoding
encoding.description: Specifies serialization details for multipart request body properties, including content-type, headers, and encoding style. Handles complex content negotiation. Note: a Content-Type header placed in the Encoding object's 'headers' map is ignored — use the 'contentType' attribute to set the MIME type for a multipart section.

style.description: Serialization style for the encoding (only applicable to multipart/form-data and application/x-www-form-urlencoded)

allowReserved.description: Allows reserved characters in field values without percent-encoding. Only applies when style is 'form' and the content type is 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'.

example.title: Example
example.description: Provides sample values for request bodies, responses, or parameters. Improves documentation clarity and enables automated testing or mocking.

value.description: Inline example value. Mutually exclusive with externalValue — only one may be set per Example.

externalValue.description: URL pointing to an external example. Mutually exclusive with value — only one may be set per Example.

externaldocumentation.title: ExternalDocumentation
externaldocumentation.description: A reference to external documentation resources (URLs, wikis, guides) that provide additional context beyond the inline API specification. Can be attached to the root OpenAPI document, individual Tags, Operations, and Schema objects.

header.title: Header
header.description: Defines a reusable HTTP header for responses or request components. The header name is derived from its map key — the OpenAPI 3.0 Header Object does not include a 'name' field (use the 'name' attribute as a local identifier only). The 'in' location is implicitly 'header' and must not be specified, distinguishing Header from Parameter.

schema.description: The schema defining the type and constraints for the header value

style.description: Serialization style for the header value. Default is 'simple' for header parameters.

explode.description: When true, array/object values generate separate header instances for each element. Default: false.

allowEmptyValue.description: Allows the header to be sent with an empty value.

allowReserved.description: Allows reserved characters in the header value without percent-encoding.

example.description: An example value for the header. Mutually exclusive with 'examples'.

examples.description: Map of named Example objects for the header. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

info.title: Info
info.description: Required root-level metadata describing the API identity, version, legal terms, and maintainer contact. The 'title' and 'version' fields are required per OpenAPI 3.0. Consumed by documentation generators, API gateways, and developer portals.

contact.description: Reference to an api.contact node providing maintainer contact details.

license.description: Reference to an api.license node specifying the API's license terms.

license.title: License
license.description: Specifies the legal license under which the API is provided, including license name and URL to full terms. Clarifies usage rights for API consumers.

link.title: Link
link.description: Describes a relationship between API responses and subsequent operations, enabling hypermedia-driven API navigation. Supports HATEOAS design patterns.

operationId.description: The operationId of the target operation within this document. Mutually exclusive with 'operationRef' — only one may be specified per Link.

operationRef.description: A relative or absolute URL reference to an operation. Mutually exclusive with 'operationId' — only one may be specified per Link.

parameters.description: Map of parameter names to runtime expressions or literal values passed to the linked operation

requestBody.description: Runtime expression or literal value for the linked operation's request body

mediatype.title: MediaType
mediatype.description: Describes the schema, examples, and encoding for a specific MIME type within a request body or response content map. Keyed by media type string (e.g., application/json).

example.description: An inline example value. Mutually exclusive with 'examples' — only one may be specified per MediaType.

oauthflow.title: OAuthFlow
oauthflow.description: Configuration for a single OAuth 2.0 grant type (implicit, password, clientCredentials, or authorizationCode). authorizationUrl is required for implicit and authorizationCode flows; tokenUrl is required for password, clientCredentials, and authorizationCode flows; scopes is required for all flows. The flow type is not an explicit field — it is implied by which attribute of the parent OAuthFlows object references this OAuthFlow.

scopes.description: Map of scope name to human-readable description string

oauthflows.title: OAuthFlows
oauthflows.description: Configuration for OAuth 2.0 authentication flows (implicit, password, clientCredentials, authorizationCode), specifying authorization URLs, token URLs, and scopes. At least one flow attribute must be specified. Always nested within a SecurityScheme where type is 'oauth2' — cannot be used independently.

openapidocument.title: OpenAPIDocument
openapidocument.description: The root document object of an OpenAPI 3.0 specification. Required fields are openapi (semver string, e.g. '3.0.3'), info, and paths. Serves as the entry point for all tooling, gateways, and documentation generators.

paths.description: Required per OpenAPI 3.0 but may be an empty object ({}) when the document is used solely to define reusable components.

security.description: Defines default security requirements applied to all operations. Individual operations can override or remove it by setting their own 'security' to an empty array ([]).

operation.title: Operation
operation.description: Defines a single HTTP method bound to a path, specifying its contract: parameters, request body, possible responses, security requirements, and tags. Operation-level security overrides global API security requirements; an empty security array ([]) explicitly removes all security enforcement for the operation. The primary unit of API surface area in OpenAPI 3.0.

operationId.description: Unique identifier for this operation across the entire API document

http_path.description: URL path template for this operation (e.g. '/api/taxonomies/{id}')

parameter.title: Parameter
parameter.description: Defines an input to an API operation by location, name, and schema. Parameters can appear in four locations: 'path' (always required, name must match the {placeholder} in the path template), 'query' (case-sensitive name, appended to the URL), 'header' (case-insensitive name), and 'cookie'. The 'schema' and 'content' attributes are mutually exclusive; 'content' requires exactly one map entry.

in.description: Location of the parameter. Path parameters must always have required: true. Default serialization style: 'form' for query/cookie, 'simple' for path/header.

style.description: Serialization style for the parameter value. Defaults: 'form' for query and cookie, 'simple' for path and header.

schema.description: The schema defining the type and constraints for the parameter value

explode.description: When true, array/object values generate separate parameters for each element. Default: true when style is 'form', false otherwise.

allowReserved.description: Allows reserved characters in query parameter values without percent-encoding. Only applies to 'query' parameters.

example.description: An example value for the parameter. Mutually exclusive with 'examples'.

examples.description: Map of named Example objects for the parameter. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

content.description: Alternative to 'schema' for complex serialization. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'; must contain exactly one map entry keyed by media type string.

pathitem.title: PathItem
pathitem.description: Groups all HTTP operations available under a single URL path pattern (e.g., /users/{userId}). Path-level parameters apply to all operations under the path and can be overridden by matching name+in at the operation level, but cannot be removed. Individual HTTP methods are represented as operation references. Supports $ref at the PathItem level, enabling shared path definitions to be reused across multiple OpenAPI specifications.

paths.title: Paths
paths.description: A map of URL path patterns to PathItem objects defining the API surface. Path keys use OpenAPI path templating (e.g., /users/{id}) and must begin with '/'. Individual path entries are modeled as relationships to api.pathitem nodes.

ratelimit.title: RateLimit
ratelimit.description: Defines throttling constraints for API or service access, specifying maximum request counts, time windows, and actions to take when limits are exceeded. Protects resources from abuse and ensures fair usage across consumers.

requests.description: Maximum number of requests permitted within the specified time window.

window.description: Duration of the rate limit window (e.g., '1m', '1h', '24h'). Requests are counted and reset per window.

scope.description: Scope at which the rate limit is applied.

action.description: Action taken when the rate limit is exceeded, per NIST SC-5 (Denial-of-Service Protection).

keyBy.description: Attribute used to identify and group requests for rate limit counting (e.g., 'user.id', 'request.ip', 'api-key').

requestbody.title: RequestBody
requestbody.description: Defines the request body for an operation as a map of MIME types to MediaType objects. The 'content' field is required and must contain at least one entry per OpenAPI 3.0. The 'required' field defaults to false. Can be placed in api.components requestBodies and referenced via $ref for reuse across multiple operations.

response.title: Response
response.description: Defines a single HTTP response returned by an operation, including its description, body content (keyed by MIME type), response headers, and optional hypermedia links. The 'content' field may be omitted entirely for responses with no body (e.g., 204 No Content). Header map keys are case-insensitive per the HTTP specification.

responses.title: Responses
responses.description: A map of HTTP status codes (e.g., 200, 404, default) to Response objects describing each possible outcome of an operation. At least one response must be defined per OpenAPI 3.0. Status code ranges (e.g., 2XX) are not supported — only specific numeric codes and 'default' are valid keys.

schema.title: Schema
schema.description: Inline data type definition using a subset of JSON Schema Draft 7, used within API operations to describe request/response data shapes. For reusable canonical types, reference data-model layer entities instead. OpenAPI 3.0 restriction: a '$ref' within a Schema ignores all sibling keywords — unlike JSON Schema where $ref can be merged with other constraints.

properties.description: Map of property names to schema definitions for object types

items.description: Schema definition for array element types

required.description: List of required property names for object types

allOf.description: Composed schema: value must be valid against all listed schemas

oneOf.description: Composed schema: value must be valid against exactly one of the listed schemas

anyOf.description: Composed schema: value must be valid against one or more of the listed schemas

$ref.description: JSON Reference to another schema definition. In OpenAPI 3.0, sibling keywords are ignored when $ref is present.

readOnly.description: Indicates the property is read-only and should not be sent in request bodies.

writeOnly.description: Indicates the property is write-only and should not be included in response bodies.

deprecated.description: Marks the schema as deprecated. Consumers should migrate away from using it.

discriminator.description: Adds support for polymorphism by specifying the property name that determines the subtype (propertyName) and an optional mapping of values to schema references.

xml.description: XML serialization metadata for this schema when producing XML representations.

externalDocs.description: Reference to an api.externaldocumentation node providing additional documentation for this schema.

securityscheme.title: SecurityScheme
securityscheme.description: Defines an authentication/authorization mechanism for the API. Supports four scheme types: apiKey (name+in required), http (scheme required), oauth2 (flows required), and openIdConnect (openIdConnectUrl required). Only 'type' is universally required. Note: SecurityScheme defines the mechanism; it is referenced by name string key in Operation.security or the root security array — those references are security requirement objects, not SecurityScheme instances.

type.description: Type of security scheme
