openIdConnectUrl.description: OpenID Connect discovery URL, required when type is openIdConnect

server.title: Server
server.description: Defines a server URL (optionally templated with {variable} placeholders) where the API is deployed. Supports multiple server entries for different environments (production, staging, dev). The 'url' field is required. A 'servers' array can appear at three levels: root document, PathItem, and Operation — each level overrides the previous for that scope.

variables.description: Map of {placeholder} names (from the url template) to api.servervariable nodes defining allowed values and defaults.

servervariable.title: ServerVariable
servervariable.description: A variable placeholder in server URL templates that can be substituted at runtime. Enables dynamic server addressing for different environments or tenants.

default.description: Required default value for the variable. When 'enum' is specified, this value must be one of the listed enum values.

enum.description: Restricts the variable to a specific set of allowed values. The 'default' value must be one of these values when enum is present. The variable name (the map key in Server.variables) must exactly match a {placeholder} token in the parent server's URL string.

tag.title: Tag
tag.description: A metadata label used to group and categorize API operations for documentation organization. Root-level Tag objects provide supplementary metadata (description, externalDocs) for tag names referenced in operations — they do not control which tags are permitted. Tags used in operations but not declared at root level are implicitly valid in OpenAPI 3.0 but will lack descriptions and externalDocs. Duplicate tag names at root level are invalid per OpenAPI 3.0.

businessactor.title: BusinessActor
businessactor.description: An active structure element representing an organizational entity (person, department, or external organization) capable of performing behavior. BusinessActors are assigned to BusinessRoles and may be associated with BusinessServices they use or provide.

businesscollaboration.title: BusinessCollaboration
businesscollaboration.description: An active structure element representing an aggregate of two or more BusinessRoles that work together to perform collective behavior (BusinessInteraction). The collaboration is meaningful only in the context of its participating roles.

businessevent.title: BusinessEvent
businessevent.description: A behavior element that represents an organizational state change, such as a customer order placed or a contract signed, that can trigger or result from a BusinessProcess or BusinessFunction.

type.description: Classification of the business event trigger

businessfunction.title: BusinessFunction
businessfunction.description: A behavior element that groups behavior based on required capabilities, skills, or resources (e.g., HR management, financial reporting). Unlike BusinessProcess, it is not goal-oriented but competency-oriented.

businessinteraction.title: BusinessInteraction
businessinteraction.description: A behavior element representing collective behavior performed by a BusinessCollaboration. It is the interaction counterpart to BusinessProcess (performed by a role) and BusinessFunction (performed by an actor).

businessinterface.title: BusinessInterface
businessinterface.description: An active structure element representing a point of access at which a BusinessService is made available to the environment, used by BusinessActors or external parties.

businessobject.title: BusinessObject
businessobject.description: A passive structure element that has relevance from a business perspective, representing information or data that active elements (actors, roles) act upon. Examples include documents, messages, contracts, and reports.

businessprocess.title: BusinessProcess
businessprocess.description: A behavior element representing a sequence or set of behaviors that achieves a specific business result for a customer or stakeholder. Driven by external triggers (BusinessEvents) or internal initiations, and performed by BusinessActors or BusinessRoles.

businessrole.title: BusinessRole
businessrole.description: An active structure element representing a named set of responsibilities, skills, or authorizations that can be assigned to a BusinessActor. Roles abstract away the concrete actor, enabling flexible actor-to-behavior assignment.

businessservice.title: BusinessService
businessservice.description: An externally visible behavior element that fulfills a business need for a customer or stakeholder. Defined from the consumer perspective; the internal realization (via BusinessProcess or BusinessFunction) is encapsulated and not exposed.

contract.title: Contract
contract.description: A passive structure element representing a formal or informal specification of an agreement between a service provider and consumer, defining obligations, rights, and terms. Often associated with a Product or BusinessService.

product.title: Product
product.description: A passive structure element representing a coherent collection of BusinessServices and Contracts offered to customers or markets. The product encapsulates the value proposition delivered to end users or business partners.

representation.title: Representation
representation.description: A passive structure element representing the perceptible form in which a BusinessObject is manifested (e.g., a printed report, a digital document, or an on-screen form). Multiple representations can carry the same BusinessObject.

format.description: The media format of this representation

accesscondition.title: AccessCondition
accesscondition.description: Defines a single boolean predicate evaluated against a request context attribute (field), comparing the runtime value using a specified operator. Composed inside a PolicyRule condition chain to form compound access control expressions.

operator.description: Comparison operator applied to the field value, per XACML 3.0 match function semantics.

accountabilityrequirement.title: AccountabilityRequirement
accountabilityrequirement.description: Expresses who is obligated to provide evidence that a specific action occurred and under what conditions that evidence can be challenged. Distinct from AuditConfig, which defines what events are captured — this node defines the obligation to prove an action took place. Implements NIST SP 800-53 AU-10 non-repudiation.

actor.title: Actor
actor.description: A named principal — human user, service account, automated system, or external entity — whose identity and trust level govern what operations it may perform. Associates with Roles for permission grouping and with SeparationOfDuty and Delegation controls, per NIST SP 800-53 AC-2 (Account Management) and SP 800-207 (Zero Trust Architecture).

type.description: Category of actor, per NIST AC-2 (Account Management) subject classification.

trustLevel.description: Zero-trust assurance level assigned to this actor, per NIST SP 800-207.

objectives.description: References nodes describing this actor's goals or intended behaviors (e.g., for threat modeling or trust analysis).

dependencies.description: References actor or delegation nodes this actor depends on for trust chain or access delegation analysis.

auditconfig.title: AuditConfig
auditconfig.description: Configuration for security audit logging, specifying what events to capture, retention periods, storage destinations, and compliance requirements. Enables security monitoring and forensic analysis.

level.description: Verbosity of audit event capture, from no logging to full payload-level tracing, per NIST SP 800-53 AU-2 Event Logging.

authenticationconfig.title: AuthenticationConfig
authenticationconfig.description: Specifies how principals are verified before accessing protected resources, including the identity provider, multi-factor requirements, session lifetime, and credential policy references. Scoped per application or service. Maps to NIST SP 800-53 IA-2, IA-5, and IA-11.

verificationLevel.description: Identity Assurance Level, per NIST SP 800-63A: IAL1 (self-asserted), IAL2 (remote identity proofing), IAL3 (in-person proofing).

bindingofduty.title: BindingOfDuty
bindingofduty.description: Obligation control requiring that a single actor who initiates a task must also complete it, preventing task hijacking or inconsistent workflow state that could be exploited by a different actor. Complements SeparationOfDuty by enforcing task continuity rather than task segregation. Anchored to NIST SP 800-53 CM-5 (Access Restrictions for Change) and workflow integrity controls.

scope.description: Organizational or operational boundary within which this binding of duty constraint applies (e.g., a project, department, or system).

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A reusable logical expression evaluated against request context (time, location, user attributes, resource state) to determine when a SecurityPolicy rule applies. Distinct from AccessCondition, which is a concrete field+operator+value predicate composed inside a PolicyRule condition chain.

operator.description: Comparison operator for the condition expression, aligned with XACML MatchType values.

evaluationType.description: When the condition is evaluated: at build time, at policy load, or at runtime during access enforcement.

countermeasure.title: Countermeasure
countermeasure.description: A security control or mitigation technique that reduces the likelihood or impact of a specific threat. Maps to NIST SP 800-53 controls by type (technical, operational, management) and tracks implementation status and residual risk.

type.description: NIST SP 800-53 control family classification: technical (hardware/software mechanisms), operational (people and procedures), management (governance), or physical (facility/environmental).

effectiveness.description: Assessed effectiveness of the countermeasure in reducing threat risk, per NIST SP 800-30 risk treatment evaluation.

encryptionRequirement.description: Minimum encryption obligation for data within this classification scheme, per NIST SP 800-53 SC-28 and SC-8 controls.

classificationLevel.description: Data sensitivity tier applied by this scheme, aligned with NIST FIPS 199 security categorization levels.

delegation.title: Delegation
delegation.description: Explicit transfer of permissions or authority from a delegator to a delegatee, subject to scope constraints and optional time bounds. Supports least-privilege enforcement (NIST AC-6) by enabling time-limited privilege elevation rather than permanent role assignment.

delegationType.description: Scope and constraints of the delegation grant, per OAuth 2.0 token delegation and NIST SP 800-162 attribute delegation models.

validUntil.description: ISO 8601 datetime after which this delegation automatically expires. Required for delegationType 'temporary' and recommended for 'conditional' to enforce time-bounded privilege grants per AC-6.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.description: An artifact that provides verifiable proof of an action or event for non-repudiation and audit purposes. Anchored to NIST SP 800-53 AU-10 (Non-repudiation), evidence types range from cryptographic signatures to plain audit log entries, with strength characterizing the assurance level per NIST SP 800-63-3.

type.description: Category of evidence artifact, per NIST AU-10 (Non-repudiation).

strength.description: Assurance strength of the evidence, per NIST SP 800-63-3 assurance levels.

source.description: Origin or location of this evidence artifact, such as a system identifier, log stream name, or storage URI.

fieldaccesscontrol.title: FieldAccessControl
fieldaccesscontrol.description: Defines access restrictions and transformation rules for a specific data field, controlling how sensitive values are masked or redacted when exposed to unauthorized principals. Used within SecureResource fieldAccess to enforce field-level data protection per NIST SP 800-53 AC-3 and SC-28.

maskingStrategy.description: Technique used to protect sensitive field values when exposed to unauthorized principals, per NIST SP 800-53 SC-28 data-at-rest protection controls.

informationentity.title: InformationEntity
informationentity.description: Named information asset subject to fine-grained access rights and data classification. Represents a logical grouping of data (e.g., customer PII, financial records) whose access is governed by associated InformationRight grants and AccountabilityRequirements, per NIST SP 800-53 AC-3 and IP-1.

classification.description: Sensitivity classification of this information asset. Use classificationLevel values: public, internal, confidential, restricted, or top-secret.

authorizationRequirements.description: References InformationRight nodes defining which actors may access this entity and under what constraints.

informationright.title: InformationRight
informationright.description: A granular access grant specifying which operations a named actor may perform on an InformationEntity, subject to a qualifying constraint expression. Implements attribute-based access control (ABAC) at the information asset level, per NIST SP 800-162 (ABAC Guide) and NIST SP 800-53 AC-3(14).

actor.description: Identifier of the actor (security.actor) to whom this right is granted.

constraint.description: Boolean constraint expression that must evaluate to true for the right to apply (e.g., 'actor.department == resource.ownerDepartment').

operations.description: Permitted operations on the information entity granted by this right.

needtoknow.title: NeedToKnow
needtoknow.description: Restricts access to a resource to only those actors whose stated purpose requires it, implementing the NIST SP 800-53 AC-3(3) mandatory access control principle. JustificationRequired enforces that actors must declare their purpose before access is granted.

objective.description: A free-text statement of the business or operational purpose that justifies access. This is a purpose declaration string, not a reference to an ActorObjective node.

passwordpolicy.title: PasswordPolicy
passwordpolicy.description: Defines password complexity, rotation, and lockout requirements for credential-based authentication. Implements NIST SP 800-53 IA-5(1) controls including minimum length, character class requirements, history enforcement, and brute-force lockout thresholds.

permission.title: Permission
permission.description: An atomic grant authorizing a specific action (e.g., read, write, execute) on a resource within a defined scope. Permissions are assigned to Roles and enforced by SecureResource access control lists. Implements NIST SP 800-53 AC-3 access enforcement and AC-6 least privilege.

scope.description: Access level granted by this permission, ranging from read-only to full administrative control.

action.description: CRUD-plus operation type this permission governs, per NIST SP 800-53 AC-3 Access Enforcement.

policyaction.title: PolicyAction
policyaction.description: The enforcement action executed when a SecurityPolicy rule condition is met. Defines the system response to a policy match (allow, deny, remediation, notification), ensuring consistent policy enforcement across the security model per NIST SP 800-53 CA-9 (Internal System Connections) and SI-3 (Malicious Code Protection).

type.description: Enforcement action type applied when the parent policy rule fires.

parameters.description: Key-value configuration for the action (e.g., redirect URL, notification recipient, masking pattern).

policyrule.title: PolicyRule
policyrule.description: An atomic policy evaluation unit consisting of a condition predicate and an enforcement effect (PERMIT or DENY), with optional obligation actions executed upon match. Multiple PolicyRules compose a SecurityPolicy evaluated in priority order.

effect.description: Policy decision outcome, per XACML 3.0 Rule element effect values.

resourceoperation.title: ResourceOperation
resourceoperation.description: A permitted operation that an actor may perform on a secured resource, forming the verb component of a subject-verb-object access control policy. Bound to AccessConditions, RateLimits, and AuditConfig nodes via the security policy model, per NIST SP 800-53 AC-3 (Access Enforcement).

operation.description: Permitted operation type on the secured resource.

conditions.description: References AccessCondition nodes that must be satisfied before this operation is authorized.

rateLimit.description: References RateLimit nodes (api.ratelimit) applied to this operation.

audit.description: References AuditConfig nodes specifying how this operation is logged for accountability.

retentionpolicy.title: RetentionPolicy
retentionpolicy.description: Defines how long security-related data (audit logs, access records, encryption keys) must be retained, archival strategies, and secure deletion procedures. Ensures compliance with regulatory requirements.

destination.description: Storage or forwarding target for retained security data, per NIST SP 800-53 AU-9 Protection of Audit Information.

deletionMethod.description: Secure disposal technique applied when data reaches end of retention period, per NIST SP 800-88 Guidelines for Media Sanitization.

role.title: Role
role.description: A named collection of Permissions implementing RBAC per NIST SP 800-53 AC-2 and AC-6. Roles can inherit from parent roles (role hierarchy), accumulating permissions transitively. The level attribute defines the role privilege tier for least-privilege enforcement.

inheritsFrom.description: Name of the parent role this role inherits permissions from. Supports single inheritance; the child accumulates all permissions of the referenced role in addition to its own.

secureresource.title: SecureResource
secureresource.description: Represents a named resource subject to security controls, defining the operations that can be performed on it and optional field-level access restrictions. Acts as the resource component in subject-action-resource access control triples per NIST SP 800-53 AC-3.

type.description: Category of protected resource, used to apply appropriate access control and audit policies per NIST SP 800-53 AC-3.

securityconstraints.title: SecurityConstraints
securityconstraints.description: An aggregate container defining the procedural access control constraints for an application, grouping SeparationOfDuty, BindingOfDuty, and NeedToKnow rules. Applied within SecurityModel to enforce AC-5 and workflow integrity controls.

securitymodel.title: SecurityModel
securitymodel.description: The root aggregate for an application's security posture, composing authentication, authorization (roles, permissions, resources), threat model, data classification, accountability requirements, and policies into a unified security specification. Scoped to a single application or bounded context.

application.description: Name or identifier of the application this security model governs. Used to scope the security posture to a single application or bounded context.

accessControlModel.description: Access control paradigm in use: DAC (discretionary), MAC (mandatory), RBAC (role-based), ABAC (attribute-based), ReBAC (relationship-based). Per NIST SP 800-162 and SP 800-207.

securitypolicy.title: SecurityPolicy
securitypolicy.description: A named, prioritized security policy containing ordered evaluation rules that determine access control decisions for targeted resources or operations. Policies are evaluated in priority order with the highest-priority matching policy determining the outcome. Implements NIST SP 800-53 AC-1 policy requirements.

target.description: The category of entity this policy governs, aligned with XACML target element semantics.

requirementLevel.description: Compliance obligation of this policy, indicating whether enforcement is required by regulation, recommended best practice, or situationally applicable.

separationofduty.title: SeparationOfDuty
separationofduty.description: Enforces NIST SP 800-53 AC-5 by requiring that sensitive tasks (e.g., request + approval, initiate + authorize) are performed by different actors or roles, preventing fraud, collusion, and undetected errors. MutuallyExclusive indicates roles that cannot be assigned to the same individual.

mutuallyExclusive.description: When true, the roles in this constraint cannot be held simultaneously by the same subject. Enforces strict role exclusivity within the separation of duty rule.

threat.title: Threat
threat.description: Represents an identified security threat with assessed likelihood and impact, targeting specific resources or controls. Linked to Countermeasures that reduce exposure. Supports NIST SP 800-53 RA-3 risk assessment and enables residual risk tracking.

likelihood.description: Probability the threat will be exploited, per NIST SP 800-30 Table E-2.

impact.description: Potential adverse impact if the threat is realized, per NIST FIPS 199.

criticality.description: Combined severity rating reflecting both likelihood and impact for prioritization.

validationrule.title: ValidationRule
validationrule.description: Specifies data validation constraints for FieldAccessControl, defining allowed patterns, value ranges, or transformations applied when accessing protected fields. Prevents data corruption and enforces field-level integrity.

type.description: Validation mechanism type, per NIST SI-10 (Information Input Validation).

value.description: Validation constraint value whose meaning depends on `type`: a regex pattern string, a numeric range expression (e.g. '1-100'), a maximum character count, a pipe-delimited list of allowed enum values, or a custom expression string.

message.description: Human-readable violation message shown to the user or logged when this validation rule fails.

severity.description: Severity of a validation rule violation.

artifact.title: Artifact
artifact.description: A piece of data that is used or produced in a software development process, or by the deployment and operation of an IT system, such as a source file, executable, script, or configuration file.

artifactType.description: Data artifact category, per ArchiMate 3.2 Artifact element.

communicationnetwork.title: CommunicationNetwork
communicationnetwork.description: A set of structures that connects nodes for the purpose of transmission, routing, and reception of data, such as a LAN, WAN, or VPN.

networkType.description: Topology and scope classification of the network, per ArchiMate 3.2 CommunicationNetwork element.

device.title: Device
device.description: A physical IT resource upon which system software and artifacts may be stored or deployed for execution, such as a server, workstation, mobile device, or IoT sensor.

deviceType.description: Physical hardware category of the device, per ArchiMate 3.2 Technology Layer device classification.

node.title: Node
node.description: A computational or physical resource that hosts, manipulates, or interacts with other computational or physical resources, such as a server cluster, virtual machine host, or container runtime.

nodeType.description: Computational resource category of the node, per ArchiMate 3.2 Node element.

path.title: Path
path.description: A link between two or more nodes through which those nodes can exchange data, representing a logical communication channel such as a network route, API connection, or message channel.

pathType.description: Transmission topology of the path, per ArchiMate 3.2 Path element.

protocol.description: Communication protocol carried by this path.

systemsoftware.title: SystemSoftware
systemsoftware.description: Software that provides or contributes to an environment for storing, executing, and using other software or data deployed within it, such as an operating system, container runtime, database engine, or middleware platform.

softwareType.description: Platform software category, per ArchiMate 3.2 SystemSoftware element.

technologycollaboration.title: TechnologyCollaboration
technologycollaboration.description: An aggregate of two or more technology active structure elements that work together to perform collective technology behavior, such as a cluster of servers, a distributed cache, or a microservice mesh.

technologyevent.title: TechnologyEvent
technologyevent.description: A technology behavior element that denotes a state change in the technology layer that triggers or results from technology behavior, such as a system alert, infrastructure notification, scheduled job trigger, or deployment completion signal.

eventType.description: Technology state change category, per ArchiMate 3.2 Technology Event element; aligned with OpenTelemetry event semantics.

technologyfunction.title: TechnologyFunction
technologyfunction.description: A collection of technology behavior that can be performed by a node, representing an internal automated capability such as data replication, load balancing, or log rotation.

technologyinteraction.title: TechnologyInteraction
technologyinteraction.description: A unit of collective technology behavior performed by two or more collaborating nodes, such as a distributed transaction, inter-service handshake, or cluster failover protocol.

technologyinterface.title: TechnologyInterface
technologyinterface.description: Point of access where technology services are made available to consuming components, per ArchiMate 3.2 TechnologyInterface. Represents the contractual boundary of a technology service — specifying the communication protocol and interaction semantics through which infrastructure capabilities are consumed. A TechnologyInterface anchors the technical coupling point between service provider and consumer within the technology layer.

protocol.description: Communication protocol exposed at this interface, per ArchiMate 3.2 TechnologyInterface element.

technologyprocess.title: TechnologyProcess
technologyprocess.description: A sequence of technology behaviors that achieves a specific technology result, such as a deployment pipeline, backup job, certificate renewal workflow, or container orchestration sequence.

technologyservice.title: TechnologyService
technologyservice.description: An externally visible unit of technology functionality exposed to other technology elements, per ArchiMate 3.2 TechnologyService. Encapsulates a discrete infrastructure capability — such as compute, storage, messaging, caching, identity, or monitoring — that is made available through a defined interface. Unlike application services, a TechnologyService operates at the infrastructure tier: it is consumed by application components or other technology services, not directly by end users or business processes.

serviceType.description: Infrastructure service category, aligned with cloud provider service classifications (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS) per ArchiMate 3.2 TechnologyService.

alert.title: Alert
alert.description: A named alerting rule that evaluates a condition against observed signals (metrics, traces, logs) on a schedule and fires a notification when the condition is met. Represents the alert definition in the monitoring backend (e.g., Prometheus alertmanager, Grafana alerting, Datadog monitors), not an individual alert event.

severity.description: Alert severity level, controlling routing and escalation policy.

condition.description: Threshold or expression that triggers the alert when true (e.g., 'p99_latency_ms > 500', 'error_rate > 0.01'). Syntax is monitoring-platform-specific.

evaluationInterval.description: How frequently the condition is evaluated (e.g., '1m', '5m', '1h'), expressed as a duration string.

notificationChannels.description: Identifiers of notification destinations (e.g., Slack channel names, PagerDuty service keys, email addresses) that receive alerts when the condition fires.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the alert detects and why it matters.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: A named monitoring dashboard that groups visualizations of APM signals (metrics, traces, logs) to provide operational visibility into system behavior. Represents the dashboard definition in the monitoring platform (e.g., Grafana, Datadog, CloudWatch), serving as the primary observability artifact teams consult during incidents and reviews.

tool.description: Monitoring platform that hosts this dashboard.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this dashboard monitors and its intended audience.

panels.description: Ordered list of visualization panels on this dashboard. Each panel displays one signal or aggregation.

title.description: Panel heading shown in the dashboard UI.

signalType.description: The APM signal type visualized by this panel.

visualizationType.description: Visualization form used to render the panel data.

exporterconfig.title: ExporterConfig
exporterconfig.description: Configuration for telemetry data export destinations, specifying protocol (OTLP, Jaeger, Prometheus), endpoints, authentication, batching, and retry policies. Controls where observability data is sent.

type.description: The type of exporter

authType.description: Authentication method used for the export destination

compression.description: Compression algorithm for OTLP export payloads. Applies to OTLP exporters only.

instrumentationconfig.title: InstrumentationConfig
instrumentationconfig.description: Configuration for OTel instrumentation of application code. Auto-instrumentation uses SDK contrib plugin libraries (e.g., opentelemetry-instrumentation-express) that hook into frameworks transparently at SDK bootstrap time. Manual instrumentation uses direct OTel API calls (Tracer.startSpan, Meter.createCounter) embedded in application code. This node controls which libraries or code paths are instrumented and whether instrumentation is enabled.

libraryName.description: OTel InstrumentationScope-compatible library name identifying the instrumented library or framework (e.g., 'io.opentelemetry.spring-webmvc-6.0'). Used to correlate this config with telemetry emitted under the matching InstrumentationScope.

instrumentationType.description: Whether instrumentation is applied automatically, manually, or both

instrumentationscope.title: InstrumentationScope
instrumentationscope.description: Named instrumented library or component that identifies the source of telemetry across all three OTel signal types (traces, metrics, logs), enabling attribution and filtering of signals by their origin.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

logconfiguration.title: LogConfiguration
logconfiguration.description: OTel LoggerProvider configuration, covering the LogRecordProcessor pipeline, LogRecordExporter wiring, and minimum severity filtering for log emission.

minimumSeverity.description: Minimum OTel SeverityText level for log records to be emitted

logprocessor.title: LogProcessor
logprocessor.description: A processing pipeline component for log records, enabling filtering, transformation, enrichment, or routing of logs before export. Customizes log processing behavior.

type.description: OTel log record processor type (simple: synchronous, batch: asynchronous buffered)

logrecord.title: LogRecord
logrecord.description: Structured log record in the OTel data model, capturing an event with dual timestamps (timeUnixNano for event occurrence, observedTimeUnixNano for ingestion), a 1–24 severity scale, and an unstructured body. Supports trace context correlation to link logs to distributed traces.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

metricconfiguration.title: MetricConfiguration
metricconfiguration.description: OTel MeterProvider-level (global) metrics SDK configuration, covering export intervals, cardinality limits, and exemplar filtering. Distinct from per-meter configuration in MeterConfig.

exportTimeoutMillis.description: Maximum time allowed for a metric export to complete

cardinalityLimit.description: Maximum number of unique metric time series per instrument

exemplarFilter.description: OTel exemplar filter controlling which exemplars are recorded

exemplarReservoirSize.description: Maximum number of exemplars retained per instrument per collection cycle. Used in conjunction with exemplarFilter to control exemplar sampling volume.

metricinstrument.title: MetricInstrument
metricinstrument.description: Defines a specific metric measurement instrument (Counter, Gauge, Histogram, etc.) with its name, unit, description, and attributes. The fundamental unit of metric collection.

type.description: OTel instrument type: synchronous (Counter, UpDownCounter, Histogram, Gauge) or asynchronous/observable (ObservableCounter, ObservableUpDownCounter, ObservableGauge)

source.description: Source code reference for code-defined metrics

resource.title: Resource
resource.description: Immutable set of attributes identifying the entity (service, host, process) that produces telemetry. Resource attributes are merged into all signals (traces, metrics, logs) emitted by a process at the SDK level, making accurate resource definition critical for cross-signal correlation.

serviceName.description: OTel Resource semantic convention: logical name of the service (service.name)

serviceVersion.description: OTel Resource semantic convention: version of the service (service.version)

deploymentEnvironment.description: OTel Resource semantic convention: deployment environment (deployment.environment)

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

span.title: Span
span.description: A named, timed unit of work in a distributed trace, as defined by the OpenTelemetry Trace Data Model. Each span is identified by a traceId+spanId pair, carries optional parent linkage (parentSpanId) for hierarchical trace composition, and is classified by SpanKind (SERVER, CLIENT, INTERNAL, PRODUCER, CONSUMER) indicating its role in the calling topology. The startTimeUnixNano and endTimeUnixNano bound the operation's timing window; statusCode (UNSET, OK, ERROR) is the canonical success/failure signal per OTel semantic conventions. Spans are produced via the OTel Tracer API, processed by SpanProcessors, and exported via SpanExporters.

spanKind.description: OTel SpanKind indicating the span's relationship to the calling and receiving system

statusCode.description: OTel span status code. UNSET = not explicitly set by instrumentation; OK = explicitly successful; ERROR = explicitly failed. Once set to OK, cannot be overridden to ERROR.

statusMessage.description: Optional human-readable error message, used when statusCode is ERROR.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

spanevent.title: SpanEvent
spanevent.description: Timestamped annotation within a span's lifetime, used to record significant moments such as exceptions (OTel semantic convention: name='exception' with exception.type, exception.message, and exception.stacktrace attributes) or state transitions.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

spanlink.title: SpanLink
spanlink.description: Non-hierarchical causality link to a span in a different trace or batch context (e.g., async message consumer linking to producer spans). Distinct from parent-child relationships, which are expressed via the parentSpanId attribute on the Span.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

traceconfiguration.title: TraceConfiguration
traceconfiguration.description: OTel TracerProvider configuration covering sampler selection, context propagation formats, and span processor pipeline. Service identity attributes (serviceName, serviceVersion, deploymentEnvironment) are OTel Resource semantic conventions and belong on the Resource node.

source.description: Source code reference for trace configuration

samplerType.description: OTel SDK sampler type controlling which traces are recorded

propagators.description: OTel context propagation formats enabled for this trace configuration

samplerParam.description: Sampling rate for ratio-based samplers (trace_id_ratio, parent_based_trace_id_ratio). Value between 0.0 (sample none) and 1.0 (sample all). Ignored for non-ratio sampler types.

spec-node.title: SpecNode
spec-node.description: Base schema for all spec node types. Each node type schema extends this with type-specific attribute constraints via allOf.

id.description: Unique node instance identifier (UUIDv4). Globally unique across the entire model.

spec_node_id.description: Reference to the spec node type that this node conforms to (e.g., 'motivation.goal')

type.description: Denormalized node type for efficient filtering and queries. Extracted from spec_node_id (the part after the dot).

layer_id.description: Denormalized layer ID for efficient layer-scoped queries. Extracted from spec_node_id (the part before the dot).

description.description: Optional detailed description of this specific node instance

attributes.description: Attribute values for this node instance. Keys must correspond to attributes defined in the per-type schema, and values must conform to the type constraints.

source_reference.description: Optional provenance tracking linking this node to source code locations

metadata.description: Optional metadata about the lifecycle and version of this node instance

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this node was created

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last modification

created_by.description: User or system that created this node

version.description: Version number of this node (incremented on updates)

spec-node-relationship.title: SpecNodeRelationship
spec-node-relationship.description: Base type for relationship type definitions in the specification. Defines valid relationships between node types, including cardinality, strength, and semantic properties.

id.description: Unique relationship ID in format: {source-layer}.{predicate}.{dest-layer}. All components use lowercase with hyphens.

source_spec_node_id.description: SpecNode ID that can serve as the source of this relationship type

source_layer.description: Denormalized layer ID extracted from source_spec_node_id for efficient queries

destination_spec_node_id.description: SpecNode ID that can serve as the destination of this relationship type

destination_layer.description: Denormalized layer ID extracted from destination_spec_node_id for efficient queries

predicate.description: Semantic predicate from PredicateCatalog describing the relationship type. Must be a valid predicate name.

cardinality.description: Multiplicity constraint on this relationship type. Defines how many source nodes can relate to how many destination nodes.

strength.description: Typical importance or strength of this relationship type

required.description: Whether instances of the source node type must have at least one relationship of this type

attributes.description: Optional schema defining attributes that can appear on relationship instances of this type

reason.description: Explanation for why this relationship exists

source-references.title: Source Code Reference Definitions
source-references.description: Reusable JSON Schema definitions for linking architecture elements to source code locations

ProvenanceType.description: How this reference was created: extracted (automated ingestion), manual (human added), inferred (pattern matching), generated (code generated from model)

SourceLocation.description: A specific location in source code, identified by file path and optional symbol

file.description: File path relative to repository root

RepositoryContext.description: Repository context for the source reference

url.description: Git remote URL (HTTPS or SSH format)

SourceReference.description: Links entity to source code location(s) with provenance tracking. For inferred or generated elements, locations may be omitted when no source file exists yet.

locations.description: One or more source code locations. Required for extracted and manual provenance; optional for inferred and generated.

attribute-spec.title: AttributeSpec Definitions
attribute-spec.description: Reusable definition for attribute specifications used in spec-level schemas (e.g., spec-node-relationship attributes)

AttributeSpec.title: AttributeSpec
AttributeSpec.description: Specification for a single attribute that can appear on node or relationship instances

type.description: The JSON type of this attribute

description.description: Human-readable description of this attribute's purpose

enum_values.description: Required when type is 'enum'. Lists the valid string values for this attribute.

format.description: Optional format constraint for string types (e.g., 'email', 'uri', 'date-time')

default.description: Default value for this attribute when not provided on a node instance. Can be any JSON value.

model-node-relationship.title: ModelNodeRelationship
model-node-relationship.description: Concrete relationship instance connecting two ModelNodes. Represents a specific relationship edge in the model graph that conforms to a SpecNodeRelationship type.

id.description: Unique relationship instance identifier (UUIDv4). Globally unique across the entire model.

spec_relationship_id.description: Reference to the SpecNodeRelationship type that this relationship conforms to (e.g., 'motivation.supports.motivation')

source_node_id.description: UUID of the source ModelNode. Must be a valid node in the model.

source_layer_id.description: Denormalized layer ID of the source node for efficient layer-scoped queries

destination_node_id.description: UUID of the destination ModelNode. Must be a valid node in the model.

destination_layer_id.description: Denormalized layer ID of the destination node for efficient layer-scoped queries

predicate.description: The semantic predicate describing this relationship (e.g., 'supports', 'realizes'). Must be a valid predicate from PredicateCatalog.

attributes.description: Optional relationship-specific attribute values. Keys must correspond to attributes defined in the SpecNodeRelationship, and values must conform to the AttributeSpec type constraints.

source_reference.description: Optional provenance tracking linking this relationship to source code locations or comments

metadata.description: Optional metadata about the lifecycle and version of this relationship instance

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this relationship was created

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last modification

created_by.description: User or system that created this relationship

version.description: Version number of this relationship (incremented on updates)

predicate-catalog.title: PredicateCatalog
predicate-catalog.description: Master catalog of all semantic predicates used in the model. Consolidates predicate definitions with their semantics, inverse relationships, and standard alignments. Single source of truth for valid predicates.

predicates.description: Mapping of predicate names to their definitions

supports.description: Element contributes to achieving or enabling another element

PredicateDefinition.title: PredicateDefinition
PredicateDefinition.description: Complete definition of a semantic predicate including its semantics and relationships

category.description: Category of relationship based on layer or domain

description.description: Human-readable description of what this predicate means

archimate_alignment.description: Corresponding ArchiMate 3.2 relationship type (if applicable), or null if none applies

semantics.description: Semantic properties describing the nature of this relationship

directionality.description: Whether the relationship is unidirectional (one-way) or bidirectional (navigable both ways)

transitivity.description: Whether the relationship is transitive: if A→B and B→C, then A→C

symmetry.description: Whether the relationship is symmetric: if A→B, then B→A

reflexivity.description: Whether an element can have this relationship with itself

default_strength.description: Default strength/importance level for relationships using this predicate

predicate-enum.title: PredicateEnum
predicate-enum.description: Enumeration of all valid predicate names from spec/schemas/base/predicates.json. This schema is dynamically generated from the predicates catalog at build time.

spec-layer.title: SpecLayer
spec-layer.description: Base type for layer definitions in the specification. Defines the structure and metadata of a layer that contains nodes and relationships.

id.description: Unique layer identifier using lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens (e.g., 'motivation', 'data-model', 'data-store')

number.description: Layer number for ordering and cross-layer references. Maps to layer position in the 12-layer model.

description.description: Purpose and scope of this layer within the architecture model
examples: Captures stakeholder concerns, goals, requirements, and constraints

inspired_by.description: External standard or model that inspired the design of this layer

node_types.description: List of SpecNode IDs that are valid in this layer. Used to define which node types can belong to this layer.

supports.description: Element contributes to achieving or enabling another element

realizes.description: Element implements or makes concrete another element

fulfills.description: Element satisfies the needs specified by another element

constrains.description: Element limits or restricts another element

governs.description: Element provides rules or guidelines for another element

owns.description: Element has responsibility or authority over another element

performs.description: Element executes or carries out another element

consumes.description: Element uses or depends on another element's functionality

covers.description: Coverage scope — test requirement or target covers an element

satisfies.description: Element fulfills or satisfies a requirement or constraint

targets.description: Threat identifies its attack surface or victim element

applies.description: Element applies a configuration or style to another element

archives-to.description: Data is archived to a storage destination

cascades-to.description: Operation or constraint cascades its effect to another element

connects.description: Network or channel connects infrastructure elements

enforces.description: Element actively enforces a policy or constraint

evaluates.description: Element evaluates a condition or expression

exposes.description: Interface exposes underlying functionality or resources

federates.description: Element participates in a federated arrangement with another element

generates.description: Element produces another element as output

governed-by.description: Element is subject to governance by another element

intercepts.description: Element intercepts or gates the flow of another element

lazy-loads.description: Element defers loading of another element until needed

manages.description: Element has management responsibility over another element

mandates.description: Element compels or requires another element

migrates.description: Element moves or transforms data to another location

optimizes.description: Element improves the performance or efficiency of another element

replicates.description: Element copies data to another element for redundancy or distribution

resolves-with.description: Navigation request is resolved using another element

tracks.description: Element records or monitors the state of another element

requires.description: Element needs another element to function correctly

protects.description: Element provides security for another element

mitigates.description: Element reduces or addresses a risk or threat

accesses.description: Element can read or modify another element

uses.description: Element depends on or utilizes another element

implements.description: Element provides concrete implementation of another element

composes.description: Whole-part relationship where part cannot exist without whole

aggregates.description: Whole-part relationship where part can exist independently

triggers.description: Element causes or initiates another element

flows-to.description: Data or control flows from one element to another

monitors.description: Element observes or tracks another element

tests.description: Element validates or verifies another element

assigned-to.description: Active element assigned to behavior or role

associated-with.description: Generic relationship indicating elements are related or connected

influence.description: Element affects or impacts another element

validates.description: Test element validates that a requirement or target is satisfied

applicationcollaboration.title: ApplicationCollaboration
applicationcollaboration.description: Aggregate of application components working together

applicationcomponent.title: ApplicationComponent
applicationcomponent.description: Modular, deployable, and replaceable part of a system

type.description: Classification of the application component kind.

applicationevent.title: ApplicationEvent
applicationevent.description: A state change in an application element that triggers reactive application behavior.

eventType.description: Classification of the application event: domain (business logic), integration (cross-system), or system (infrastructure/runtime).

applicationfunction.title: ApplicationFunction
applicationfunction.description: Automated behavior performed by an application component for internal purposes, not directly exposed as a service to external consumers.

applicationinteraction.title: ApplicationInteraction
applicationinteraction.description: Collective application behavior performed by two or more application components working in collaboration, representing their joint behavioral contribution.

pattern.description: Interaction pattern used by this application collaboration.

applicationinterface.title: ApplicationInterface
applicationinterface.description: Point of access where application service is available

protocol.description: Application-level communication protocol used to expose the service. Use application-level protocols only; network/transport-level protocols (TCP, TLS) belong in the Technology Layer.

applicationprocess.title: ApplicationProcess
applicationprocess.description: An ordered sequence of application behaviors performed by an application component to achieve a specific operational result.

serviceType.description: Classification of how the service exposes its functionality to consumers.

dataobject.title: DataObject
dataobject.description: A passive application element representing data structured for automated processing by application components. Unlike behavioral elements, it is accessed, manipulated, or stored by active elements — not an actor itself. For canonical data schema definitions shared across layers, use the Data Model layer instead.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.description: Outcome of analysis of the state of affairs of the enterprise or any part of it, and its environment. Assessments commonly take the form of strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, or threats (SWOT).

assessmentType.description: Classification of the assessment method or framework used

constraint.title: Constraint
constraint.description: Restriction on the freedom of design and implementation choices available when realizing a system, as opposed to requirements which must be satisfied.

constraintType.description: Category of constraint restricting the solution space

driver.title: Driver
driver.description: External or internal condition that motivates an organization to change its goals, strategy, or architecture.

category.description: Category of the motivating condition or force

goal.title: Goal
goal.description: High-level statement of intent, direction, or desired end state

meaning.title: Meaning
meaning.description: Knowledge or expertise present in, or the interpretation given to, a concept in a particular context, as understood by stakeholders.

outcome.title: Outcome
outcome.description: End result intended or already achieved by the organization or a system, distinct from a Goal which expresses the desired direction.

principle.title: Principle
principle.description: Normative property of all systems in a given context, or a statement governing how an organization intends to fulfill its mission and guide decision-making.

category.description: Domain area to which this architectural principle applies

requirement.title: Requirement
requirement.description: Statement of need that must be realized by a system, component, or solution, and that can be associated with stakeholders, goals, or constraints.

requirementType.description: Classification of what aspect of the system this requirement addresses

stakeholder.title: Stakeholder
stakeholder.description: Individual, team, or organization that has an interest in, or is affected by, the effects of the architecture.

type.description: Classification of the stakeholder's relationship to the organization

value.title: Value
value.description: Relative worth, utility, or importance of a concept, phenomenon, or outcome to one or more stakeholders.

valueType.description: Category of worth or utility this value represents
